title.zh: 卷四十七 班梁列傳
title.raw: 卷四十七 班梁列傳 Volume 47: Biographies of Ban, Liang

sentences.zh: 班超字仲升，扶風平陵人，徐令彪之少子也。

translations.literal: Ban Chao, courtesy name Zhongsheng, was a man of Pingling in Fufeng, the youngest son of Ban Biao, who had been magistrate of Xu.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao, style Zhongsheng, came from Pingling in Fufeng commandery; he was the youngest son of Ban Biao, once prefect of Xu.

sentences.zh: 為人有大志，不修細節。

translations.literal: In his conduct he had great ambitions and did not polish petty details.
translations.idiomatic: He aimed high and did not fuss over trifling points of deportment.

sentences.zh: 然內孝謹，居家常執勤苦，不恥勞辱。

translations.literal: Yet inwardly he was filial and careful; at home he constantly took on toil and hardship and was not ashamed of labor or humiliation.
translations.idiomatic: Within the family he was dutiful and meticulous, shouldered the hardest chores at home without complaint, and thought nothing of drudgery or lowly work.

sentences.zh: 有口辯，而涉獵書傳。

translations.literal: He had eloquence of speech and broadly perused texts and commentaries.
translations.idiomatic: He was a fluent speaker and had read widely in the classics and histories.

sentences.zh: [1]永平五年，兄固被召詣校書郎，[2]超與母隨至洛陽。

translations.literal: [1] In the fifth year of Yongping, his elder brother Ban Gu was summoned to serve as collator in the palace library; [2] Chao accompanied his mother and went to Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: [1] In Yongping 5 (62 CE), his older brother Ban Gu was called to the capital as a court collator; [2] Ban Chao traveled with their mother and settled in Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 家貧，常為官傭書以供養。

translations.literal: The family was poor, and he constantly was hired by the government to copy documents in order to supply their maintenance.
translations.idiomatic: Because the household was poor, he often earned their keep by copying documents on contract for the clerks.

sentences.zh: 久勞苦，嘗輟業投筆歎曰：「大丈夫無它志略，猶當效傅介子、張騫立功異域，以取封侯，安能久事筆研閒乎？

translations.literal: After long toil and hardship, he once stopped his work, threw down his brush, and sighed, saying, "If a great man has no other plan or strategy, he ought still to imitate Fu Jiezi and Zhang Qian in establishing merit in strange lands and thereby win a marquisate—how can he long serve among brush and inkstone?"
translations.idiomatic: Weary after long days of grinding work, he once set down his brush mid-copy and exclaimed, "A man of spirit who lacks some grand design should still follow Fu Jiezi and Zhang Qian—win glory beyond the frontier and earn a noble title. Why should I spend my life chained to a desk?"

sentences.zh: 」[3]左右皆笑之。

translations.literal: " [3] Those around him all laughed at him.
translations.idiomatic: " [3] Everyone within earshot laughed.

sentences.zh: 超曰：「小子安知壯士志哉！

translations.literal: Chao said, "How can petty fellows know the mind of a stalwart!"
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao shot back, "Small minds cannot fathom what drives a man of resolve!"

sentences.zh: 」其後行詣相者，曰：「祭酒，布衣諸生耳，[4]而當封侯萬里之外。

translations.literal: " Later he went to a physiognomist, who said, "Libationer, you are only a common-clothed student, [4] yet you will be enfeoffed as marquis beyond ten thousand li."
translations.idiomatic: " Later he had his features read. The diviner told him, "Sir, you are nothing but a poor scholar in plain cloth, [4] yet your fate is a marquisate thousands of miles from here."

sentences.zh: 」超問其狀。

translations.literal: " Chao asked what form it would take.
translations.idiomatic: " Ban Chao pressed him for particulars.

sentences.zh: 相者指曰：「生燕頷虎頸，飛而食肉，此萬里侯相也。

translations.literal: The physiognomist pointed and said, "You have a swallow's jaw and a tiger's neck; you fly and eat flesh—this is the physiognomy of a marquis of ten thousand li."
translations.idiomatic: The man traced his features and said, "Swallow's jowl, tiger's neck—you are built to rise fast and feed on rich fare. That is the face of a frontier marquis."

sentences.zh: 」久之，顯宗問固「卿弟安在」，固對「為官寫書，受直以養老母」。

translations.literal: " After a long while, Emperor Xianzong asked Ban Gu, "Where is your younger brother?" Gu replied, "He copies documents for the government and receives wages to support his aged mother."
translations.idiomatic: " Time passed. Emperor Ming (Xianzong) asked Ban Gu where his brother was. Ban Gu answered that Ban Chao copied texts for the clerks and used the pay to support their mother.

sentences.zh: 帝乃除超為蘭台令史，[5]後坐事免官。

translations.literal: The emperor thereupon appointed Chao as secretary in the Orchid Terrace office; [5] later, because of an affair, he was dismissed from office.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then named him a Lan Terrace clerk; [5] he was later stripped of his post over an incident.

sentences.zh: 十六年，奉車都尉竇固出擊匈奴，以超為假司馬，將兵別擊伊吾，戰於蒲類海，多斬首虜而還。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year, Commandant of the Imperial Coach Dou Gu went out to strike the Xiongnu; he made Chao acting major and sent him with troops separately to attack Yiwu; they fought at the Pulei Sea, beheaded many of the enemy, and returned.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixteenth year of Yongping (73 CE), Dou Gu, commandant of the imperial coach, marched against the Xiongnu. He gave Ban Chao the brevet rank of major and detached him to strike Yiwu. They fought at Lake Barkol, took many heads, and withdrew.

sentences.zh: [6]固以為能，遣與從事郭恂俱使西域。

translations.literal: [6] Gu considered him capable and sent him together with adjutant Guo Xun as envoys to the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: [6] Dou Gu, impressed by his ability, dispatched him to the Western Regions in company with Guo Xun, the staff adjutant.

sentences.zh: 超到鄯善，[7]鄯善王廣奉超禮敬甚備，後忽更疏懈。

translations.literal: When Chao arrived in Shanshan, [7] King Guang of Shanshan treated Chao with rites and respect that were very complete, but afterward suddenly became more distant and slack.
translations.idiomatic: When Ban Chao reached Shanshan, [7] King Guang first received him with every courtesy; then, without warning, his manner turned cold and distant.

sentences.zh: 超謂其官屬曰：「寧覺廣禮意薄乎？

translations.literal: Chao said to his officials, "Have you not noticed that Guang's courteous intent has grown thin?"
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao asked his men, "Surely you have seen how thin Guang's hospitality has become?"

sentences.zh: 此必有北虜使來，狐疑未知所從故也。

translations.literal: This must be because envoys from the northern barbarians have come, and he hesitates, not knowing whom to follow.
translations.idiomatic: There must be a Xiongnu embassy in town; he is wavering between two masters.

sentences.zh: 明者睹未萌，況已著邪。」

translations.literal: A perceptive man sees what has not yet sprouted—how much more when it is already manifest!"
translations.idiomatic: The wise read trouble before it breaks—how much more when the signs are already plain!"

sentences.zh: 乃召侍胡詐之曰：「匈奴使來數日，今安在乎？

translations.literal: He then summoned a Hu attendant and deceived him, saying, "The Xiongnu envoys have been here several days—where are they now?"
translations.idiomatic: He called in a native attendant and bluffed him: "The Xiongnu delegation has been here for days. Where are they hiding?"

sentences.zh: 」侍胡惶恐，具服其狀。

translations.literal: " The Hu attendant was frightened and in full detail confessed the situation.
translations.idiomatic: " Cornered, the man panicked and told him everything.

sentences.zh: 超乃閉侍胡，悉會其吏士三十六人，與共飲，酒酣，因激怒之曰：「卿曹與我俱在絕域，[8]欲立大功，以求富貴。

translations.literal: Chao then shut up the Hu attendant, assembled all his clerks and soldiers—thirty-six men—and drank with them; when the wine was heated, he therefore stirred them to anger, saying, "You gentlemen and I are together in a cut-off land, [8] wishing to establish great merit in order to seek wealth and honor.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao locked the attendant away, gathered his entire party of thirty-six clerks and troops, and threw a feast. When they were deep in their cups, he roused them: "You and I are stranded at the ends of the earth, [8] yet we still mean to win fame and fortune."

sentences.zh: 今虜使到裁數日，而王廣禮敬即廢；

translations.literal: Now the barbarian envoys have arrived only a few days, yet King Guang's courteous respect has already been cast aside;
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu envoys have been here only a handful of days, and already King Guang has dropped every courtesy toward us;

sentences.zh: 如令鄯善收吾屬送匈奴，骸骨長為豺狼食矣。

translations.literal: if Shanshan were to seize our persons and deliver us to the Xiongnu, our bones would long be food for jackals and wolves.
translations.idiomatic: if Shanshan hands us over to the Xiongnu, our bones will feed the wolves of the desert.

sentences.zh: 為之柰何？

translations.literal: What is to be done about this?"
translations.idiomatic: What are we going to do?"

sentences.zh: 」官屬皆曰：「今在危亡之地，死生從司馬。

translations.literal: " The officials all said, "Now we are in a place of peril and extinction; in life or death we follow the major."
translations.idiomatic: " They answered as one: "We stand on the edge of ruin; our lives are yours, Major."

sentences.zh: 」超曰：「不入虎穴，不得虎子。

translations.literal: " Chao said, "If one does not enter the tiger's lair, one cannot obtain the tiger cub.
translations.idiomatic: " Ban Chao said, "No one takes a tiger cub without entering the den.

sentences.zh: 當今之計，獨有因夜以火攻虜，使彼不知我多少，必大震怖，可殄盡也。

translations.literal: As for the plan for the present moment, there is only to use the night to attack the barbarians with fire, so that they will not know how many we are; they will certainly be greatly shaken with terror and can be exterminated to the last.
translations.idiomatic: Our only move is a night raid with fire. If they cannot count us, panic will break them, and we can wipe them out.

sentences.zh: 滅此虜，則鄯善破膽，功成事立矣。

translations.literal: Destroy these barbarians, then Shanshan will be broken in courage, and merit will be achieved and the affair established.
translations.idiomatic: Destroy that embassy, and Shanshan's nerve will fail; the deed will be done and our names made."

sentences.zh: 」觿曰：「當與從事議之。

translations.literal: " Guo Xun said, "We ought to consult the adjutant about this."
translations.idiomatic: " Guo Xun objected: "We must take this to the adjutant first."

sentences.zh: 」超怒曰：「吉凶決於今日。

translations.literal: " Chao angrily said, "Good or ill fortune is decided today.
translations.idiomatic: " Ban Chao flared: "Our fate is sealed tonight—no tomorrow about it.

sentences.zh: 從事文俗吏，聞此必恐而謀洩，死無所名，非壯士也！

translations.literal: The adjutant is a clerkish vulgar official; if he hears this he will certainly be frightened and the plan will leak; dying would win no name—he is no stalwart!"
translations.idiomatic: The adjutant is a timid desk-man; one word to him and he will panic, the plot will leak, and we will die for nothing. That is not how bold men act!"

translations.literal: " Guo Xun said, "Good."
translations.idiomatic: " Guo Xun yielded: "Very well."

sentences.zh: 初夜，遂將吏士往奔虜營。

translations.literal: At the first watch of the night, he then led clerks and soldiers and ran to assault the barbarian camp.
translations.idiomatic: At first watch he led his men at a run against the Xiongnu camp.

sentences.zh: 會天大風，超令十人持鼓藏虜捨後，約曰：「見火然，皆當鳴鼓大呼。

translations.literal: It happened that heaven sent a great wind; Chao ordered ten men to take drums and hide behind the barbarians' quarters, agreeing, "When you see fire blaze, all shall beat the drums and shout loudly.
translations.idiomatic: A fierce wind sprang up. Ban Chao hid ten men with drums behind the enemy compound and told them, "The moment you see flame, raise a thunder of drums and war cries.

sentences.zh: 」餘人悉持兵弩夾門而伏。

translations.literal: " The rest all grasped weapons and crossbows and lay in ambush flanking the gate.
translations.idiomatic: " The rest took arms and lay in wait on both sides of the gate.

sentences.zh: 超乃順風縱火，前後鼓噪。

translations.literal: Chao then, following the wind, set loose fire; front and rear drummed and clamored.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao lit the camp downwind; drums and shouts erupted front and rear.

sentences.zh: 虜觿驚亂，超手格殺三人，吏兵斬其使及從士三十餘級，余觿百許人悉燒死。

translations.literal: The barbarian host was alarmed and in confusion; Chao with his own hands struck and killed three men; clerk-soldiers beheaded their envoy and more than thirty ranks of followers; the remaining host of about a hundred or more were all burned to death.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu camp dissolved in panic. Ban Chao killed three men himself; his troops cut down the envoy and over thirty of his escort, and the rest of the party—some hundred souls—perished in the flames.

sentences.zh: [9]明日乃還告郭恂，恂大驚，既而色動。

translations.literal: [9] On the morrow he then returned and reported to Guo Xun; Xun was greatly alarmed, and presently his expression shifted.
translations.idiomatic: [9] Next morning Ban Chao told Guo Xun. Guo was stunned, then his face changed as greed flickered across it.

sentences.zh: 超知其意，舉手曰：「掾雖不行，班超何心獨擅之乎？

translations.literal: Chao knew his intent and, raising his hand, said, "Though the adjutant did not go forth, how could Ban Chao's heart alone monopolize the merit?"
translations.idiomatic: Reading his thoughts, Ban Chao raised a hand and said, "You were not in the fight, sir—would I dare hoard the credit?"

sentences.zh: 」恂乃悅。

translations.literal: " Xun thereupon was pleased.
translations.idiomatic: " Guo Xun brightened at once.

sentences.zh: 超於是召鄯善王廣，以虜使首示之，一國震怖。

translations.literal: Chao thereupon summoned King Guang of Shanshan and showed him the heads of the barbarian envoys; the whole state was shaken with terror.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao summoned King Guang and set the severed heads of the Xiongnu envoys before him. The court of Shanshan trembled with fear.

sentences.zh: 超曉告撫慰，遂納子為質。

translations.literal: Chao enlightened him with announcement and soothing comfort, and Guang then sent a son as hostage.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao explained Han's intentions and offered reassurance; the king sent a prince to Luoyang as hostage.

sentences.zh: 還奏於竇固，固大喜，具上超功效，並求更選使使西域。

translations.literal: Returning, he reported to Dou Gu; Gu was greatly pleased, fully memorialized Chao's merit and achievement, and requested to select anew an envoy to send to the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: On their return Dou Gu was elated. He drafted a full memorial on Ban Chao's exploit and asked the throne to appoint a fresh mission to the west.

sentences.zh: 帝壯超節，詔固曰：「吏如班超，何故不遣而更選乎？

translations.literal: The emperor admired Chao's integrity and issued an edict to Dou Gu, saying, "When there is an official like Ban Chao, why not dispatch him and instead select another?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor, impressed by Ban Chao's steadfastness, instructed Dou Gu: "With such an officer already in the field, why look elsewhere for an envoy?"

sentences.zh: 今以超為軍司馬，令遂前功。

translations.literal: Now appoint Chao as army major and order him to carry forward his previous merit."
translations.idiomatic: Appoint him army major and let him carry the enterprise forward."

sentences.zh: 」超復受使，固欲益其兵，超曰：「願將本所從三十餘人足矣。

translations.literal: " Chao again received the mission; Gu wished to increase his troops, but Chao said, "I wish to lead the more than thirty men who originally followed me—that is sufficient.
translations.idiomatic: " Ban Chao accepted the new commission. When Dou Gu offered reinforcements, he replied, "Give me the thirty-odd men who came west with me; that is enough.

sentences.zh: 如有不虞，多益為累。」

translations.literal: If there should be unforeseen events, more would only be a burden."
translations.idiomatic: In a crisis, extra mouths would only slow us down."

sentences.zh: 是時于窴王廣德新攻破莎車，遂雄張南道，[10]而匈奴遣使監護其國。

translations.literal: At this time King Guangde of Yutian had newly defeated and broken Kasha, and thereupon lorded it over the southern route, [10] while the Xiongnu sent envoys to supervise and protect his state.
translations.idiomatic: King Guangde of Khotan had just crushed Kasha and dominated the southern track, [10] while Xiongnu overseers watched his court.

sentences.zh: 超既西，先至于窴。

translations.literal: When Chao had gone west, he first arrived in Yutian.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao's westward road led him first to Khotan.

sentences.zh: 廣德禮意甚疏。

translations.literal: Guangde's courteous intent was very distant.
translations.idiomatic: Guangde received him coolly.

sentences.zh: 且其俗信巫。

translations.literal: Moreover their custom trusted shamans.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom put great stock in its shamans.

sentences.zh: 巫言：「神怒何故欲向漢？

translations.literal: The shaman said, "The spirits are angry—why do you wish to turn toward Han?"
translations.idiomatic: The royal shaman announced, "The gods are wroth—why do you lean toward Han?"

sentences.zh: 漢使有騧馬，急求取以祠我。

translations.literal: The Han envoy has a dapple-gray horse; urgently seek it and take it to sacrifice to me.
translations.idiomatic: The Han envoy rides a dapple-gray—seize it at once for my altar."

sentences.zh: 」廣德乃遣使就超請馬。

translations.literal: " Guangde thereupon sent envoys to Chao to request the horse.
translations.idiomatic: " Guangde sent men to demand the horse from Ban Chao.

sentences.zh: [11]超密知其狀，報許之，而令巫自來取馬。

translations.literal: [11] Chao secretly knew the situation, reported consent, but ordered the shaman himself to come take the horse.
translations.idiomatic: [11] Ban Chao saw through the ruse, agreed aloud, and told the shaman to fetch the mount in person.

sentences.zh: 有頃，巫至，超即斬其首以送廣德，因辭讓之。

translations.literal: After a little while, when the shaman arrived, Chao immediately beheaded him and sent the head to Guangde, then rebuked him with reproof.
translations.idiomatic: When the shaman appeared, Ban Chao struck off his head and had it delivered to Guangde with a stern rebuke.

sentences.zh: 廣德素聞超在鄯善誅滅虜使，大惶恐，即攻殺匈奴使者而降超。

translations.literal: Guangde had long heard that Chao at Shanshan had executed and destroyed the barbarian envoys; he was greatly frightened and terrorized, immediately attacked and killed the Xiongnu envoys, and submitted to Chao.
translations.idiomatic: Guangde already knew how Ban Chao had annihilated the Xiongnu embassy at Shanshan; shaking with fear, he murdered the Xiongnu resident ministers and submitted to Han through Ban Chao.

sentences.zh: 超重賜其王以下，因鎮撫焉。

translations.literal: Chao heavily rewarded those from the king downward and thereby pacified and comforted them.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao heaped gifts on the king and his nobles and brought the country quietly under Han influence.

sentences.zh: 時龜茲王建為匈奴所立，倚恃虜威，據有北道，攻破疏勒，殺其王，[12]而立龜茲人兜題為疏勒王。

translations.literal: At the time King Jian of Qiuci had been set up by the Xiongnu; relying on barbarian might, he occupied the northern route, defeated and broke Shule, killed its king, [12] and established Dou Ti, a man of Qiuci, as king of Shule.
translations.idiomatic: King Jian of Kucha owed his throne to the Xiongnu. Backed by their power he held the northern road, overran Shule, executed its king, [12] and installed Dou Ti, a Kuchean, as ruler of Shule.

sentences.zh: 明年春，超從閒道至疏勒。

translations.literal: The next spring, Chao by a bypath arrived in Shule.
translations.idiomatic: The following spring Ban Chao slipped along a side route into Shule.

sentences.zh: 去兜題所居盤橐城九十里，逆遣吏田慮先往降之。

translations.literal: Ninety li from Pancheng where Dou Ti dwelled, he sent forward in advance clerk Tian Lü to go first and summon his submission.
translations.idiomatic: He halted ninety li short of Dou Ti's stronghold at Pantuo and sent Tian Lü ahead to treat for surrender.

sentences.zh: □慮曰：「兜題本非疏勒種，國人必不用命。

translations.literal: Tian Lü said, "Dou Ti was originally not of Shule stock; the people of the state will certainly not obey his commands.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Lü warned him, "Dou Ti is no son of Shule; the nobles will not obey him.

sentences.zh: 若不即降，便可執之。

translations.literal: If he does not immediately submit, then you may seize him.
translations.idiomatic: If he will not yield at once, seize him by force."

sentences.zh: 」慮既到，兜題見慮輕弱，殊無降意。

translations.literal: " When Lü had arrived, Dou Ti saw that Lü was slight and weak and had absolutely no intent to submit.
translations.idiomatic: " When Tian Lü arrived, Dou Ti sized him up as slight and harmless and showed no inclination to capitulate.

sentences.zh: 慮因其無備，遂前劫縛兜題。

translations.literal: Lü, because he was unprepared, thereupon advanced and seized and bound Dou Ti.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Lü caught him off guard, rushed forward, and bound him.

sentences.zh: 左右出其不意，皆驚懼奔走。

translations.literal: Those left and right, caught unawares, all were alarmed and frightened and fled running.
translations.idiomatic: His attendants, taken by surprise, panicked and scattered.

sentences.zh: 慮馳報超，超即赴之，悉召疏勒將吏，說以龜茲無道之狀，因立其故王兄子忠為王，[13]國人大悅。

translations.literal: Lü galloped to report to Chao; Chao immediately hastened there, fully summoned the generals and clerks of Shule, explained the lawless condition of Qiuci, and thereupon established Zhong, the son of his former king's elder brother, as king; [13] the people of the state were greatly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Lü raced back with word; Ban Chao rode in at once, assembled Shule's commanders, denounced Kucha's tyranny, and enthroned Zhong, nephew of the murdered king; [13] the realm rejoiced.

sentences.zh: 忠及官屬皆請殺兜題，超不聽，欲示以威信，釋而遣之。

translations.literal: Zhong and the officials all requested to kill Dou Ti, but Chao did not listen, wishing to display authority and good faith; he released him and sent him away.
translations.idiomatic: Zhong and his courtiers begged to execute Dou Ti, but Ban Chao refused: he meant to awe them with magnanimity, so he freed Dou Ti and sent him home.

sentences.zh: 疏勒由是與龜茲結怨。

translations.literal: Shule from this thereby formed enmity with Qiuci.
translations.idiomatic: From that day Shule and Kucha were sworn enemies.

sentences.zh: 建初三年，超率疏勒、康居、于窴、拘彌兵一萬人攻姑墨石城，破之，[17]斬首七百級。

translations.literal: In the third year of Jianchu, Chao led troops of Shule, Kangju, Yutian, and Jumi—ten thousand men—to attack the stone fortress of Gumo, broke it, [17] and beheaded seven hundred ranks.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 3 (78 CE) Ban Chao led ten thousand men from Shule, Kangju, Khotan, and Jumi against Gumo's stone citadel, stormed it, [17] and took seven hundred heads.

sentences.zh: 超欲因此叵平諸國，[18]乃上疏請兵。

translations.literal: Chao wished by this means thereby to pacify the various states; [18] he therefore submitted a memorial requesting troops.
translations.idiomatic: He meant to ride that victory to pacify the Tarim states [18] and memorialized Luoyang for reinforcements.

sentences.zh: 曰：「臣竊見先帝欲開西域，故北擊匈奴，西使外國，鄯善、于窴實時向化。

translations.literal: It said: "Your servant has privately seen that the former emperor wished to open the Western Regions; therefore he struck the Xiongnu in the north and sent envoys west to foreign states, and Shanshan and Yutian in truth at that time turned toward civilization.
translations.idiomatic: He wrote: "I have reflected that our late sovereign meant to open the Western Regions—hence the northern campaigns against the Xiongnu and embassies sent west—while Shanshan and Khotan were among the first to accept Han guidance.

sentences.zh: 今拘彌、莎車、疏勒、月氏、烏孫、康居復願歸附，欲共並力破滅龜茲，平通漢道。

translations.literal: Now Jumi, Kasha, Shule, the Yuezhi, Wusun, and Kangju again wish to submit and attach themselves, wishing together jointly to exert strength to destroy and extinguish Qiuci and level and open the Han road.
translations.idiomatic: Today Jumi, Kasha, Shule, the Yuezhi, Wusun, and Kangju again ask to renew their allegiance and join arms to crush Kucha and clear the road to the interior.

sentences.zh: 若得龜茲，則西域未服者百分之一耳。

translations.literal: If Qiuci can be obtained, then of those in the Western Regions not yet submissive there will be only one in a hundred.
translations.idiomatic: Take Kucha, and the handful of states still outside our fold shrinks to one percent of the west.

sentences.zh: 臣伏自惟念，卒伍小吏，實願從谷吉效命絕域，庶幾張騫□身曠野。

translations.literal: Your servant has bowed and privately reflected: a petty clerk from the ranks, he truly wishes to follow Gu Ji in rendering his life in the cut-off regions, hoping perhaps to be like Zhang Qian, who abandoned his body in the wild wastes.
translations.idiomatic: I am only a foot soldier turned clerk, yet I would follow Gu Ji in laying down my life beyond the passes, and dare hope to match Zhang Qian, who risked everything on the open steppe.

sentences.zh: [19]昔魏絳列國大夫，尚能和輯諸戎，[20]況臣奉大漢之威，而無鈆刀一割之用乎？

translations.literal: [19] Formerly Wei Jiang was a grandee of a feudal state, yet still could harmonize and gather the various Rong; [20] how much more your servant, bearing the majesty of great Han, yet having no use like a lead knife's single cut?"
translations.idiomatic: [19] Wei Jiang was merely a statesman of one kingdom, yet he reconciled the Rong tribes; [20] shall I, bearing the prestige of Han, be less useful than a blunt blade's single stroke?"

sentences.zh: [21]前世議者皆曰取三十六國，號為斷匈奴右臂。

translations.literal: [21] Discussers of former ages all said that taking the thirty-six states was called cutting off the Xiongnu's right arm.
translations.idiomatic: [21] Strategists of old agreed: win the thirty-six kingdoms and you sever the Xiongnu's right arm.

sentences.zh: [22]今西域諸國，自日之所入，莫不向化，[23]大小欣欣，貢奉不絕，唯焉耆、龜茲獨未服從。

translations.literal: [22] Now the various states of the Western Regions, from where the sun enters, none do not turn toward civilization; [23] great and small are joyful, tribute offerings unceasing—only Yanqi and Qiuci alone have not yet submitted and obeyed.
translations.idiomatic: [22] From the sunset lands westward every kingdom inclines toward us; [23] great and small send gifts without cease. Only Yanqi and Kucha still hold out.

sentences.zh: 臣前與官屬三十六人奉使絕域，備遭艱□。

translations.literal: Your servant formerly, with officials and subordinates thirty-six persons, received a mission to the cut-off regions and fully encountered hardship and peril.
translations.idiomatic: When I first led thirty-six men into the farthest west, we tasted every hardship the desert could offer.

sentences.zh: 自孤守疏勒，於今五載，胡夷情數，臣頗識之。

translations.literal: From solitary guarding of Shule down to the present five years, the dispositions and stratagems of the Hu and Yi, your servant has somewhat come to know them.
translations.idiomatic: Five years alone in Shule have taught me the temper of the frontier peoples.

sentences.zh: 問其城郭小大，皆言『倚漢與依天等』。

translations.literal: Asked concerning their walled cities great or small, they all say, 'Relying on Han is equal to relying on Heaven.'"
translations.idiomatic: Ask any ruler, great or small, and you hear the same refrain: 'Leaning on Han is like leaning on Heaven itself.'"

sentences.zh: 以是效之，則□領可通，[24]□領通則龜茲可伐。

translations.literal: By this tested, then the Pamir passes can be opened; [24] if the Pamir passes are opened, then Qiuci can be attacked.
translations.idiomatic: The lesson is plain: open the Congling passes [24] and a strike at Kucha becomes possible.

sentences.zh: 今宜拜龜茲侍子白霸為其國王，以步騎數百送之，與諸國連兵，歲月之閒，龜茲可禽。

translations.literal: Now it is fitting to invest Ba Ba, the Qiuci palace hostage, as king of that state, send him with several hundred infantry and cavalry, join weapons with the various states, and within the space of months or years Qiuci can be captured.
translations.idiomatic: Invest Ba Ba, Kucha's hostage prince, as king, escort him with a few hundred horse and foot, weld the oases into one host, and Kucha can be taken within a year or two.

sentences.zh: 以夷狄攻夷狄，計之善者也。

translations.literal: Using the Yi and Di to attack the Yi and Di is the best of plans.
translations.idiomatic: Pit barbarian against barbarian—that is the soundest strategy.

sentences.zh: [25]臣見莎車、疏勒田地肥廣，草牧饒衍，不比敦煌，鄯善閒也，[26]兵可不費中國而徹食自足。

translations.literal: [25] Your servant has seen that the fields of Kasha and Shule are fertile and broad, grass and pasturage abundant and overflowing, not comparable to the region between Dunhuang and Shanshan; [26] troops need not expense the interior of China but provisions and food are self-sufficient.
translations.idiomatic: [25] Kasha and Shule own rich soil and ample pasture—nothing like the barren strip between Dunhuang and Shanshan; [26] an army there can live off the land without draining the interior.

sentences.zh: 且姑墨、溫宿二王，特為龜茲所置，[27]既非其種，更相厭苦，其埶必有降反。

translations.literal: Moreover the two kings of Gumo and Wensu were specially installed by Qiuci; [27] since they are not of its stock, they mutually detest and suffer one another; their situation must bring surrender or revolt.
translations.idiomatic: The kings of Gumo and Wensu are Kucha's puppets; [27] foreign to the people they oppress, they loathe one another and will crack or defect.

sentences.zh: 若二國來降，則龜茲自破。

translations.literal: If the two states come to surrender, then Qiuci of itself will be broken.
translations.idiomatic: Win those two oases and Kucha collapses of its own weight.

sentences.zh: 願下臣章，參考行事。

translations.literal: I beg that your servant's memorial be sent down and consulted in carrying out affairs.
translations.idiomatic: I ask that this memorial be laid before the ministers for deliberation.

sentences.zh: 誠有萬分，死復何恨。

translations.literal: If there truly be one ten-thousandth part of truth, what regret even in death?
translations.idiomatic: If there is the slightest merit in my plea, I shall die content.

sentences.zh: 臣超區區，特蒙神靈，竊冀未便僵仆，目見西域平定，陛下舉萬年之觴，[28]薦勳祖廟，布大喜於天下。

translations.literal: Your servant Chao is insignificant, yet specially receives spiritual blessing; he privately hopes not yet to fall stiff and dead, to see with his own eyes the Western Regions pacified, your majesty raising the goblet of ten thousand years, [28] presenting merit at the ancestral temple, and spreading great joy throughout the realm.
translations.idiomatic: I am a minor man heaven has spared; I pray I may not fall before I see the west at peace, Your Majesty toasting ten thousand years, [28] the ancestral shrines recording our triumph, and joy spreading through the empire.

sentences.zh: 」[29]書奏，帝知其功可成，議欲給兵。

translations.literal: " [29] When the memorial was presented, the emperor knew his merit could be accomplished and deliberated wishing to supply troops.
translations.idiomatic: " [29] The memorial reached the throne; the emperor saw the plan could work and the court began debating reinforcements.

sentences.zh: 平陵人徐干素與超同志，上疏願奮身佐超。

translations.literal: Xu Gan of Pingling had long been of one mind with Chao; he submitted a memorial wishing to exert his body to assist Chao.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Gan of Pingling, who had long shared Ban Chao's purpose, offered his own memorial volunteering to join him.

sentences.zh: 五年，遂以干為假司馬，將□刑及義從千人就超。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, thereupon Gan was made acting major and led reprieved convicts and voluntary followers, a thousand men, to join Chao.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year of Jianchu (80 CE) Xu Gan was named brevet major and marched a thousand reprieved convicts and volunteers to Ban Chao's camp.

sentences.zh: 先是莎車以為漢兵不出，遂降於龜茲，而疏勒都尉番辰[30]亦復反叛。

translations.literal: Earlier Kasha, because it thought Han troops would not come out, thereupon submitted to Qiuci, while the Shule commandant Fan Chen [30] also again rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile Kasha, assuming no Han army would march west, had gone over to Kucha, and Fan Chen, [30] Shule's commandant, had mutinied as well.

sentences.zh: 會徐干適至，超遂與干擊番辰，大破之，斬首千餘級，多獲生口。

translations.literal: It happened that Xu Gan had just arrived; Chao thereupon with Gan struck Fan Chen, greatly defeated him, beheaded more than a thousand ranks, and captured many living prisoners.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Gan arrived in the nick of time; together they fell on Fan Chen, shattered his force, took more than a thousand heads, and rounded up many captives.

sentences.zh: 超既破番辰，欲進攻龜茲。

translations.literal: When Chao had defeated Fan Chen, he wished to advance and attack Qiuci.
translations.idiomatic: With Fan Chen crushed, Ban Chao prepared to march on Kucha.

sentences.zh: 以烏孫兵強，宜因其力，乃上言：「烏孫大國，控弦十萬，故武帝妻以公主，[31]至孝宣皇帝，卒得其用。

translations.literal: Because Wusun's troops were strong, it was fitting to use their strength; he therefore submitted words: "Wusun is a great state; those who draw the bowstring are a hundred thousand; therefore Emperor Wu married a princess to it; [31] down to Emperor Xiaoxuan, in the end its strength was obtained for use.
translations.idiomatic: Wusun's cavalry was formidable; he memorialized that the union should be renewed: "Wusun fields a hundred thousand archers; Emperor Wu married a princess into their royal house; [31] under Emperor Xuan we reaped the reward."

sentences.zh: [32]今可遣使招慰，與共合力。

translations.literal: [32] Now envoys can be sent to summon and comfort them, joining with them in combined strength."
translations.idiomatic: [32] Send envoys now to court them and bind them to our campaign."

sentences.zh: 」帝納之。

translations.literal: " The emperor accepted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved the proposal.

sentences.zh: 八年，拜超為將兵長史，假鼓吹幢麾。

translations.literal: In the eighth year, Chao was appointed chief clerk with command of troops and was lent drums, pipes, banners, and command flags.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year of Jianchu he was named chief clerk with military authority and given the regalia of a field commander—drums, pipes, banners, and standards.

sentences.zh: [33]以徐干為軍司馬，別遣□候李邑護送烏孫使者，賜大小昆彌以下錦帛。

translations.literal: [33] Xu Gan was made army major; separately a military prefect, Li Yi, was dispatched to escort the Wusun envoys, and brocades and silks were bestowed from the great and lesser Kunmi downward.
translations.idiomatic: [33] Xu Gan was promoted to army major; Li Yi, a junhou, was sent separately to escort the Wusun party, bearing brocades and silks as gifts for the great and lesser Kunmi and their nobles.

translations.literal: [34] The transmitted text marks an editorial note here without added wording.
translations.idiomatic: [34] The edition carries only this annotation marker at this point in the narrative.

sentences.zh: 李邑始到于窴，而值龜茲攻疏勒，恐懼不敢前，因上書陳西域之功不可成，又盛毀超擁愛妻，抱愛子，安樂外國，無內顧心。

translations.literal: Li Yi had just reached Yutian when it happened that Qiuci was attacking Shule; in fear he did not dare advance, and therefore submitted a memorial stating that achievement in the Western Regions could not be completed, and moreover heaped slander on Chao for cherishing a beloved wife and embracing a beloved child, finding ease and pleasure in a foreign land, with no mind to look homeward.
translations.idiomatic: Li Yi had barely reached Khotan when he learned Kucha was besieging Shule. Frightened, he halted and memorialized that the western enterprise was hopeless, while vilifying Ban Chao as a man dallying abroad with wife and child, indifferent to home.

sentences.zh: 超聞之，歎曰：「身非曾參而有三至之讒，恐見疑於當時矣。

translations.literal: When Chao heard it, he sighed and said, "My person is not Zeng Shen, yet there is slander thrice repeated—I fear I will be suspected in my own time."
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao sighed when the news reached him: "I am no Zeng Shen, yet I face the old tale of thrice-told slander—I will be distrusted at court."

sentences.zh: 」[35]遂去其妻。

translations.literal: " [35] He thereupon sent away his wife.
translations.idiomatic: [35] He dismissed his wife to silence the gossip.

sentences.zh: 帝知超忠，乃切責邑曰：「縱超擁愛妻，抱愛子，思歸之士千餘人，何能盡與超同心乎？

translations.literal: The emperor knew Chao was loyal and therefore sternly rebuked Yi, saying, "Even if Chao cherishes a beloved wife and embraces a beloved child, how can more than a thousand men who long to return all be of one mind with Chao?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor, knowing Ban Chao's loyalty, rebuked Li Yi sharply: "Even if Ban Chao kept wife and child with him, could more than a thousand homesick soldiers all share his single-minded devotion?"

sentences.zh: 」令邑詣超受節度。

translations.literal: " He ordered Yi to go to Chao and receive his orders.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered Li Yi to report to Ban Chao and take his orders."

sentences.zh: 詔超：「若邑任在外者，便留與從事。」

translations.literal: An edict to Chao: "If Yi is fit for duty abroad, then retain him with the adjutant."
translations.idiomatic: The throne told Ban Chao: "If Li Yi proves useful in the field, keep him under your adjutant."

sentences.zh: 超即遣邑將烏孫侍子還京師。

translations.literal: Chao immediately sent Yi to lead the Wusun palace hostage back to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao at once sent Li Yi to escort the Wusun hostage prince to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 徐干謂超曰：「邑前親毀君，欲敗西域，今何不緣詔書留之，更遣它吏送侍子乎？

translations.literal: Xu Gan said to Chao, "Yi formerly in person slandered you and wished to ruin the Western Regions—why not rely on the edict's wording to detain him and send another officer to escort the hostage?"
translations.idiomatic: Xu Gan asked, "Li Yi slandered you to wreck the mission—why not use the edict's loophole, keep him here, and send someone else with the prince?"

sentences.zh: 」超曰：「是何言之陋也！

translations.literal: " Chao said, "How base is that way of speaking!"
translations.idiomatic: " Ban Chao replied, "That would be petty beyond words."

sentences.zh: 以邑毀超，故今遣之。

translations.literal: Because Yi slandered Chao, therefore now he is sent away.
translations.idiomatic: Precisely because he maligned me, I send him back to show my clear conscience.

sentences.zh: 內省不疚，何恤人言！

translations.literal: Inward examination finds no fault—why heed others' words!"
translations.idiomatic: When your heart is clean, why fear rumor?"

sentences.zh: [36]快意留之，非忠臣也。」

translations.literal: [36] To gratify one's feelings by detaining him is not the conduct of a loyal minister."
translations.idiomatic: [36] To keep him out of spite would be unworthy of a loyal servant."

sentences.zh: 明年，復遣假司馬和恭等四人將兵八百詣超，超因發疏勒、于窴兵擊莎車。

translations.literal: The next year, acting majors He Gong and four others were again dispatched leading eight hundred troops to join Chao; Chao thereupon mobilized troops of Shule and Yutian to strike Kasha.
translations.idiomatic: The following year four brevet majors led by He Gong marched eight hundred men to Ban Chao, who then raised Shule and Khotan forces against Kasha.

sentences.zh: 莎車陰通使疏勒王忠，啖以重利，[37]忠遂反從之，西保烏即城。

translations.literal: Kasha secretly sent envoys to King Zhong of Shule and tempted him with heavy profit; [37] Zhong thereupon rebelled and followed them, withdrawing west to hold Wuji city.
translations.idiomatic: Kasha secretly bribed King Zhong of Shule with rich promises; [37] Zhong turned traitor and withdrew west to fortify Wuji.

sentences.zh: 超乃更立其府丞成大為疏勒王，悉發其不反者以攻忠。

translations.literal: Chao thereupon replaced him by establishing Cheng Da, the chief clerk of his administration, as king of Shule, and fully mobilized those who had not rebelled to attack Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao deposed Zhong and enthroned Cheng Da, the kingdom's chief clerk, then marched every loyal Shule regiment against the rebel.

sentences.zh: 積半歲，而康居遣精兵救之，超不能下。

translations.literal: For half a year they piled up effort, but Kangju sent picked troops to rescue him, and Chao could not take him.
translations.idiomatic: Six months of siege failed when Kangju threw in elite reinforcements.

sentences.zh: 是時月氏新與康居婚，相親，超乃使使多繼錦帛遺月氏王，令曉示康居王，康居王乃罷兵，執忠以歸其國，烏即城遂降於超。

translations.literal: At this time the Yuezhi had newly intermarried with Kangju and were intimate; Chao therefore sent envoys carrying large bundles of brocade and silk as gifts to the Yuezhi king, ordering him to enlighten the king of Kangju; the king of Kangju thereupon dismissed his troops, seized Zhong and returned him to his state, and Wuji city thereupon submitted to Chao.
translations.idiomatic: The Yuezhi had just allied with Kangju by marriage. Ban Chao sent rich silks to the Yuezhi king and had him persuade the Kangju ruler to withdraw. Kangju pulled out, handed Zhong back to his own people, and Wuji opened its gates to Han.

sentences.zh: 後三年，忠說康居王借兵，還據損中，[38]密與龜茲謀，遣使詐降於超。

translations.literal: After three years, Zhong persuaded the king of Kangju to lend troops, returned and occupied Sunzhong, [38] secretly plotted with Qiuci, and sent envoys feigning submission to Chao.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later Zhong borrowed Kangju troops, reoccupied Sunzhong, [38] conspired with Kucha in secret, and sent envoys offering a false surrender to Ban Chao.

sentences.zh: 超內知其奸而外偽許之。

translations.literal: Chao inwardly knew his treachery but outwardly feigned consent.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao saw through the ruse but played along.

sentences.zh: 忠大喜，即從輕騎詣超。

translations.literal: Zhong was greatly pleased and immediately with light cavalry went to Chao.
translations.idiomatic: Zhong, elated, rode in with a light escort to Ban Chao's camp.

sentences.zh: 超密勒兵待之，為供張設樂。

translations.literal: Chao secretly arrayed troops awaiting him and for him spread provisions and set up music.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao hid armed men in readiness and staged a banquet with music.

sentences.zh: [39]酒行，乃叱吏縛忠斬之。

translations.literal: [39] When the wine had gone round, he then shouted to clerks to bind Zhong and behead him.
translations.idiomatic: [39] Mid-feast he gave the signal; his guards seized Zhong and struck off his head.

sentences.zh: 因擊破其觿，殺七百餘人，南道於是遂通。

translations.literal: He thereupon struck and broke their host, killing more than seven hundred men; the southern route from this was then opened.
translations.idiomatic: He then routed Zhong's force, killing over seven hundred, and the southern road lay open again.

sentences.zh: 明年，超發于窴諸國兵二萬五千人，復擊莎車。

translations.literal: The next year, Chao mobilized twenty-five thousand troops from Yutian and the various states and again struck Kasha.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he raised twenty-five thousand men from Khotan and allied kingdoms for a second drive on Kasha.

sentences.zh: 而龜茲王遣左將軍發溫宿、姑墨、尉頭合五萬人救之。

translations.literal: But the king of Qiuci sent the general of the left to mobilize Wensu, Gumo, and Weitou, in all fifty thousand men, to rescue it.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Kucha sent his left general to levy fifty thousand troops from Wensu, Gumo, and Weitou to relieve the city.

sentences.zh: 超召將校及于窴王議曰：「今兵少不敵，其計莫若各散去。

translations.literal: Chao summoned generals, colonels, and the king of Yutian and deliberated, saying, "Now our troops are few and cannot match them; in planning, nothing is better than that each side disperse and depart.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao called his officers and the king of Khotan to council: "We are outnumbered; our best ruse is to pretend to break camp and scatter."

sentences.zh: 于窴從是而東，長史亦於此西歸，可須夜鼓聲而發。

translations.literal: Yutian from here goes east, and the chief clerk also from here returns west; we may wait for the drum at night and then set out.
translations.idiomatic: Let Khotan march east while I, the chief clerk, withdraw west; we move on the night drum."

sentences.zh: 」陰緩所得生口。

translations.literal: " Secretly he relaxed the captured persons he had taken.
translations.idiomatic: He quietly loosened the guard on his prisoners so word would leak.

sentences.zh: 龜茲王聞之大喜，自以萬騎於西界遮超，溫宿王將八千騎於東界徼于窴。

translations.literal: When the king of Qiuci heard it he was greatly pleased; he himself with ten thousand horse at the western border intercepted Chao, while the king of Wensu led eight thousand horse at the eastern border to intercept Yutian.
translations.idiomatic: The Kucha king swallowed the bait with delight, posting ten thousand cavalry on the western approaches to trap Ban Chao while the king of Wensu took eight thousand horsemen eastward to cut off Khotan.

sentences.zh: 超知二虜已出，密召諸部勒兵，雞鳴馳赴莎車營，胡大驚亂奔走，追斬五千餘級，大獲其馬畜財物。

translations.literal: When Chao knew the two barbarian forces had gone out, he secretly summoned the various divisions to array troops; at cockcrow he galloped to the Kasha camp; the Hu were greatly alarmed and fled in confusion; pursuing them, his men beheaded more than five thousand ranks and greatly captured their horses, livestock, and goods.
translations.idiomatic: Once both enemy columns had marched off, Ban Chao mustered his men, rode at cockcrow straight into the Kasha camp, threw the allies into panic, and ran them down for more than five thousand heads, seizing vast herds and baggage.

sentences.zh: 莎車遂降，龜茲等因各退散，自是威震西域。

translations.literal: Kasha thereupon submitted, and Qiuci and the others therefore each withdrew and scattered; from this his might shook the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: Kasha capitulated; Kucha and its allies melted away. From that day Ban Chao's name struck terror across the Tarim basin.

sentences.zh: 初，月氏嘗助漢擊車師有功，是歲貢奉珍寶、符拔、師子，[40]因求漢公主。

translations.literal: Earlier, the Yuezhi had once assisted Han in striking Cheshi with merit; in this year they presented tribute of precious objects, fu-bo horses, and lions, [40] and thereby requested a Han princess.
translations.idiomatic: The Yuezhi had once helped Han crush Cheshi. That year they sent tribute—jewels, fabo horses, lions [40]—and asked for a Han princess in marriage.

sentences.zh: 超拒還其使，由是怨恨。

translations.literal: Chao refused and sent back their envoys; from this they harbored resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao turned the embassy away, and the court of the Kushans nursed a grudge.

sentences.zh: 永元二年，月氏遣其副王謝將兵七萬攻超。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yongyuan, the Yuezhi sent their vice-king Xie leading seventy thousand troops to attack Chao.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 2 (90 CE) the Yuezhi deputy king Xie marched seventy thousand men against Ban Chao.

sentences.zh: 超觿少，皆大恐。

translations.literal: Chao's host was few; all were greatly frightened.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao's command was tiny by comparison, and his men were terrified.

sentences.zh: 超譬軍士曰：「月氏兵雖多，然數千里踰□領來，非有運輸，何足憂邪？

translations.literal: Chao instructed the soldiers, saying, "Though Yuezhi troops are many, yet having crossed the Pamir passes from several thousand li away, they have no supply transport—what is there to worry about?"
translations.idiomatic: He rallied them: "Their numbers look vast, but they have crossed thousands of li over the Pamirs with no baggage train behind them—why should we fear?"

sentences.zh: 但當收谷堅守，彼饑窮自降，不過數十日決矣。

translations.literal: We need only gather grain and hold firm on defense; they, hungry and exhausted, will of themselves submit—it will be settled within no more than several tens of days.
translations.idiomatic: Harvest the grain, hold the walls, and hunger will break them inside a month."

sentences.zh: 」謝遂前攻超，不下，又鈔掠無所得。

translations.literal: " Xie thereupon advanced and attacked Chao but could not take him, and moreover raided and plundered without obtaining anything.
translations.idiomatic: " Xie pressed the siege without success and found nothing to forage.

sentences.zh: 超度其糧將盡，必從龜茲求救，乃遣兵數百於東界要之。

translations.literal: Chao calculated that their grain would soon be exhausted and that they would certainly seek rescue from Qiuci; he therefore sent several hundred troops on the eastern border to intercept them.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao reckoned their supplies were nearly gone and that they would beg Kucha for relief; he posted a few hundred men on the eastern road to ambush the convoy.

sentences.zh: 謝果遣騎繼金銀珠玉以賂龜茲。

translations.literal: Xie indeed sent cavalry carrying gold, silver, pearls, and jade to bribe Qiuci.
translations.idiomatic: Xie sent riders laden with gold, silver, and gems to buy Kucha's aid.

sentences.zh: 超伏兵遮擊，盡殺之，持其使首以示謝。

translations.literal: Chao ambushed troops to intercept and strike them, killing them all to the last, and took the heads of their envoys to show Xie.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao's ambush wiped out the party; he sent the bearers' heads to Xie's camp.

sentences.zh: 謝大驚，即遣使請罪，願得生歸。

translations.literal: Xie was greatly alarmed and immediately sent envoys to beg pardon, wishing to obtain life and return.
translations.idiomatic: Xie was shaken and sued for peace, begging leave to withdraw alive.

sentences.zh: 超縱遣之。

translations.literal: Chao released and sent them away.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao let them go.

sentences.zh: 月氏由是大震，歲奉貢獻。

translations.literal: The Yuezhi from this were greatly shaken and each year presented tribute offerings.
translations.idiomatic: The Kushan court was thoroughly cowed and sent annual tribute thereafter.

sentences.zh: 明年，龜茲、姑墨、溫宿皆降，乃以超為都護，徐干為長史。

translations.literal: The next year, Qiuci, Gumo, and Wensu all submitted; thereupon Chao was made protector-general and Xu Gan chief clerk.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Kucha, Gumo, and Wensu capitulated; Ban Chao was named protector-general of the Western Regions and Xu Gan his chief clerk.

sentences.zh: 拜白霸為龜茲王，遣司馬姚光送之。

translations.literal: Ba Ba was invested as king of Qiuci, and major Yao Guang was dispatched to escort him.
translations.idiomatic: Ba Ba was enthroned as king of Kucha, escorted by Major Yao Guang.

sentences.zh: 超與光共脅龜茲廢其王尤利多而立白霸，使光將尤利多還詣京師。

translations.literal: Chao together with Guang jointly coerced Qiuci to depose its king Youliduo and establish Ba Ba, and ordered Guang to lead Youliduo back to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao and Yao Guang forced Kucha to depose King Youliduo in favor of Ba Ba, then sent Youliduo to Luoyang under escort.

sentences.zh: 超居龜茲它干城，徐干屯疏勒。

translations.literal: Chao dwelt at Tagan city in Qiuci; Xu Gan was stationed in Shule.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao made his headquarters at Tagan in Kucha while Xu Gan held Shule.

sentences.zh: 西域唯焉耆、危須、尉犁以前沒都護，懷二心，其餘悉定。

translations.literal: In the Western Regions only Yanqi, Weixu, and Yuli, which formerly had destroyed the protector-general, harbored divided hearts; the rest were all settled.
translations.idiomatic: Only Yanqi, Weixu, and Yuli—kingdoms that had murdered an earlier protector-general—still wavered; every other state acknowledged Han.

sentences.zh: 六年秋，超遂發龜茲、鄯善等八國兵合七萬人，及吏士賈客千四百人討焉耆。

translations.literal: In the autumn of the sixth year, Chao thereupon mobilized troops of Qiuci, Shanshan, and eight states in all seventy thousand men, together with clerks, soldiers, and merchants fourteen hundred persons, to punish Yanqi.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn, in the sixth year of Yongyuan (94 CE), he raised seventy thousand men from Kucha, Shanshan, and six other kingdoms, plus fourteen hundred clerks, troops, and merchants, for a punitive expedition against Yanqi.

sentences.zh: 兵到尉犁界，而遣曉說焉耆、尉犁、危須曰：「都護來者，欲鎮撫三國。

translations.literal: When the troops reached the border of Yuli, he sent men to enlighten Yanqi, Yuli, and Weixu, saying, "The protector-general has come wishing to pacify and comfort the three states.
translations.idiomatic: At the Yuli frontier he sent heralds to Yanqi, Yuli, and Weixu: "The protector-general comes to reassure your three kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 即欲改過向善，宜遣大人來迎，當賞賜王侯已下，[41]事畢即還。

translations.literal: If you wish at once to reform faults and turn toward good, it is fitting to send great men to come welcome him; he will reward and bestow from kings and marquises downward, [41] and when the affair is finished he will at once return.
translations.idiomatic: If you mean to mend your ways, send your leading nobles to meet him; he will reward kings and officers alike [41] and withdraw when the business is done.

sentences.zh: 今賜王彩五百匹。」

translations.literal: Now he bestows on the king five hundred bolts of colored silk."
translations.idiomatic: He sends five hundred bolts of brocade as a gift to your king."

sentences.zh: 焉耆王廣遣其左將北鞬支奉牛酒迎超。

translations.literal: King Guang of Yanqi sent his general of the left, Beijianzhi, presenting oxen and wine to welcome Chao.
translations.idiomatic: King Guang of Yanqi sent his left general Beijianzhi with cattle and wine to greet Ban Chao.

sentences.zh: [42]超結鞬支曰：「汝雖匈奴侍子，而今秉國之權。

translations.literal: [42] Chao bound Beijianzhi with obligation, saying, "Though you are a Xiongnu palace hostage, now you wield the state's power.
translations.idiomatic: [42] Ban Chao took Beijianzhi aside: "You may be a Xiongnu hostage by birth, yet you hold real power in Yanqi.

sentences.zh: 都護自來，王不以時迎，皆汝罪也。

translations.literal: The protector-general has come in person; that the king did not welcome him in due time is all your crime.
translations.idiomatic: The protector-general is here in person, and your king failed to meet him—that fault is yours."

sentences.zh: 」或謂超可便殺之。

translations.literal: " Some told Chao he could at once kill him.
translations.idiomatic: Some urged Ban Chao to kill him on the spot.

sentences.zh: 超曰：「非汝所及。

translations.literal: Chao said, "That is not what you can reach to.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao said, "You miss the point.

sentences.zh: 此人權重於王，今未入其國而殺之，遂令自疑，設備守險，豈得到其城下哉！

translations.literal: This man's authority weighs more than the king's; if now, before entering his state, we kill him, it will make them suspect themselves, set up defenses and guard narrows—how could we then reach below their walls!"
translations.idiomatic: He outranks the king. Execute him before we cross the border and they will bar every pass—how then do we reach their capital?"

sentences.zh: 」於是賜而遣之。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he rewarded him and sent him away.
translations.idiomatic: He heaped gifts on Beijianzhi and sent him back.

sentences.zh: 廣乃與大人迎超於尉犁，奉獻珍物。

translations.literal: Guang thereupon with great men welcomed Chao at Yuli and presented tribute of precious objects.
translations.idiomatic: Guang then came with his nobles to Yuli, where he received Ban Chao and offered rare gifts.

sentences.zh: 焉耆國有葦橋之險，廣乃絕橋，不欲令漢軍入國。

translations.literal: The state of Yanqi had the peril of a reed bridge; Guang thereupon cut the bridge, not wishing to let Han troops enter the state.
translations.idiomatic: Yanqi's reed bridge was the choke point; Guang had it destroyed to keep the Han army out.

sentences.zh: 超更從它道厲度。

translations.literal: Chao changed to another route and crossed with care.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao forded by another, harder crossing.

sentences.zh: [43]七月晦，到焉耆，去城二十里，*

translations.literal: [43] On the last day of the seventh month he reached Yanqi, twenty li from the city; *
translations.idiomatic: [43] On the last day of the seventh month he drew up twenty li short of the walls of Yanqi; *

translations.literal: (The transmitted text inserts a collation mark here without narrative sense.)
translations.idiomatic: (The edition carries an editorial collation marker at this break in the sentence.)

sentences.zh: *營大澤中。

translations.literal: * he encamped in a great marsh.
translations.idiomatic: * and pitched camp in a broad marsh.

sentences.zh: 廣出不意，大恐，乃欲悉驅其人共入山保。

translations.literal: Guang, caught unawares, was greatly frightened and wished to drive all his people together into the mountains to hold fast.
translations.idiomatic: Guang, caught off guard, panicked and planned to herd his entire population into the hills for refuge.

sentences.zh: 焉耆左候元孟先嘗質京師，密遣使以事告超，超即斬之，示不信用。

translations.literal: The left hou of Yanqi, Yuan Meng, had formerly once been a hostage in the capital; he secretly sent an envoy to report the affair to Chao; Chao immediately beheaded him, showing that he did not trust or employ him.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Meng, the left hou of Yanqi, who had once been a hostage in Luoyang, sent a secret messenger to Ban Chao; Ban Chao executed the man at once to prove he would not be played.

sentences.zh: 乃期大會諸國王，因揚聲當重加賞賜，於是焉耆王廣、尉犁王泛及北鞬支等三十人相率詣超。

translations.literal: He then scheduled a great assembly of the various states' kings and on that pretext loudly declared that he would heavily add rewards and bestowals; thereupon King Guang of Yanqi, King Fan of Yuli, and Beijianzhi and others thirty persons in a body went to Chao.
translations.idiomatic: He summoned the kings of the region to a great conclave, promising lavish rewards; King Guang of Yanqi, King Fan of Yuli, Beijianzhi, and some thirty others walked into his camp together.

sentences.zh: 其國相腹久等十七人懼誅，皆亡入海，[44]而危須王亦不至。

translations.literal: Their state chancellor Fujiu and seventeen others, fearing execution, all fled to the sea; [44] moreover the king of Weixu also did not arrive.
translations.idiomatic: Seventeen ministers led by Chancellor Fujiu fled toward the coast, fearing execution; [44] the king of Weixu stayed away entirely.

sentences.zh: 坐定，超怒詰廣曰：「危須王何故不到？

translations.literal: When they were seated, Chao angrily questioned Guang, saying, "Why did the king of Weixu not come?"
translations.idiomatic: Once they were seated, Ban Chao turned on Guang in fury: "Why is the king of Weixu absent?"

sentences.zh: 腹久等所緣逃亡？

translations.literal: What was the reason Fujiu and the others fled?"
translations.idiomatic: Why have Fujiu and his party run away?"

sentences.zh: 」遂叱吏士收廣、泛等於陳睦故城斬之，傳首京師。

translations.literal: " He then shouted to clerks and soldiers to seize Guang, Fan, and the others at the old city of Chen Mu, behead them, and send the heads to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered his men to drag Guang, Fan, and their party to the ruins of Chen Mu's old fort, struck off their heads, and sent the heads to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 因縱兵鈔掠，斬首五千餘級，獲生口萬五千人，馬畜牛羊三十餘萬頭，更立元孟為焉耆王。

translations.literal: He thereupon unleashed troops to raid and plunder, beheading more than five thousand ranks, capturing fifteen thousand living persons, and horses, livestock, cattle, and sheep more than three hundred thousand head; he replaced the king by establishing Yuan Meng as king of Yanqi.
translations.idiomatic: His troops then swept the countryside, taking more than five thousand heads, fifteen thousand captives, and three hundred thousand head of horse and herd stock, and enthroned Yuan Meng as king of Yanqi.

sentences.zh: 超留焉耆半歲，慰撫之。

translations.literal: Chao remained in Yanqi half a year, comforting and soothing them.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao stayed six months in Yanqi to restore order.

sentences.zh: 於是西域五十餘國悉皆納質內屬焉。

translations.literal: Thereupon more than fifty states of the Western Regions all sent hostages and submitted inwardly.
translations.idiomatic: More than fifty western kingdoms then sent hostages and acknowledged Han sovereignty.

sentences.zh: 明年，下詔曰：「往者匈奴獨擅西域，寇盜河西，永平之末，城門晝閉。

translations.literal: The next year an edict was issued, saying, "In former times the Xiongnu alone monopolized the Western Regions, raided and robbed Hexi; at the end of Yongping, the city gates were closed by day.
translations.idiomatic: The following year an edict ran: "Once the Xiongnu held the Western Regions to themselves and ravaged Hexi; in the last years of Yongping the capital barred its gates even in daylight.

sentences.zh: 先帝深愍邊萌嬰羅寇害，乃命將帥擊右地，破白山，臨蒲類，[45]取車師，城郭諸國震懾響應，遂開西域，置都護。

translations.literal: The former emperor deeply pitied the border sprouts who suffered the harm of invaders; he therefore ordered generals and commanders to strike the right region, break the White Mountain, approach Pulei, [45] take Cheshi; the walled states of the various countries were shaken with fear and answered like echoes, and thus the Western Regions were opened and a protector-general was installed.
translations.idiomatic: Our late sovereign pitied the frontier folk caught in that storm and sent armies against the western Xiongnu, broke the White Mountain tribes, reached Lake Barkol, [45] and took Cheshi; the oasis states trembled and rallied to Han, the road west reopened, and a protector-general took his seat.

sentences.zh: 而焉耆王舜、舜子忠獨謀悖逆，持其險隘，覆沒都護，並及吏士。

translations.literal: But King Shun of Yanqi and Shun's son Zhong alone plotted rebellion and inversion, relying on their perilous narrows, overturned and destroyed the protector-general, together with reaching clerks and soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: Yet King Shun of Yanqi and his son Zhong turned traitor, used their mountain defiles to ambush the protector-general, and slaughtered him and his command.

sentences.zh: 先帝重元元之命，憚兵役之興，故使軍司馬班超安集于窴以西。

translations.literal: The former emperor valued the lives of the black-haired common people and feared the rise of military service; therefore he sent army major Ban Chao to pacify and gather the region west of Yutian.
translations.idiomatic: The late emperor shrank from another general war yet would not abandon the people, so he sent Army Major Ban Chao to pacify everything west of Khotan.

sentences.zh: 超遂踰□領，迄縣度，[46]出入二十二年，莫不賓從。

translations.literal: Chao thereupon crossed the Pamir passes, reaching as far as Xiandu, [46] going out and in twenty-two years; none did not submit and follow.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao crossed the Pamirs to the hanging crossings beyond, [46] and for twenty-two years of campaigning every kingdom along the way submitted.

sentences.zh: 改立其王，而綏其人。

translations.literal: He replaced and established their kings and thereby soothed their people.
translations.idiomatic: He deposed tyrants, enthroned rightful rulers, and brought calm to their peoples.

sentences.zh: 不動中國，不煩戎士，得遠夷之和，同異俗之心，而致天誅，蠲宿恥，以報將士之讎。

translations.literal: Without moving the interior of China, without troubling border troops, he obtained the harmony of distant Yi, united the hearts of different customs, and thereby brought down heaven's punishment, wiped away long-standing shame, and thereby avenged the officers and soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: He did it without draining the interior or drafting fresh legions from the border: he won the trust of distant tribes, reconciled alien customs to Han, executed heaven's judgment on the murderers, washed away an old disgrace, and avenged our fallen generals.

sentences.zh: [47]司馬法曰：『賞不踰月，欲人速鶯為善之利也。

translations.literal: [47] The Sima fa says: 'Rewards do not pass beyond the month, wishing men swiftly to perceive the profit of doing good.
translations.idiomatic: [47] The Simafa says, 'Bestow rewards within the month so men quickly see the good of virtuous deeds.'

sentences.zh: 』其封超為定遠侯，邑千戶。

translations.literal: ' Let Chao be enfeoffed as marquis of Dingyuan, with a fief of one thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: ' Let Ban Chao be enfeoffed as Marquis of Dingyuan with a thousand-household fief.'"

translations.literal: " [48] The transmitted text ends the quoted edict with this annotation marker.
translations.idiomatic: " [48] The edict closes with this citation marker in the received text.

sentences.zh: 超自以久在絕域，年老思土。

translations.literal: Chao himself considered that he had long been in cut-off regions and, old in years, longed for his native soil.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao felt the weight of years spent at the rim of the world and longed for home.

sentences.zh: 十二年，上疏曰：「臣聞太公封齊，五世葬周，狐死首丘，代馬依風。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year he submitted a memorial, saying, "Your servant has heard that the Grand Duke, enfeoffed in Qi, for five generations was buried in Zhou; the fox dies with its head toward its mound; the Dai horse leans with the wind.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth year of Yongyuan he memorialized: "I have read how the Grand Duke's line, though lords of Qi, still sought tombs in Zhou; how the fox turns its head toward its burrow at death; how northern horses still scent the home wind."

sentences.zh: [49]夫周齊同在中土千里之閒，況於遠處絕域，小臣能無依風首丘之思哉？

translations.literal: [49] Now Zhou and Qi alike lay within the central land within a thousand li—how much more when one is located in distant cut-off regions—can a petty minister lack the thought of leaning with the wind and turning his head toward his mound?"
translations.idiomatic: [49] Qi and Zhou lay within the same heartland, a mere thousand li apart—how much more must a humble servant beyond the passes ache for the wind from home?"

sentences.zh: 蠻夷之俗，畏壯侮老。

translations.literal: The custom of the Man and Yi is to fear the strong and insult the old.
translations.idiomatic: Barbarian ways respect only strength and despise age.

sentences.zh: [50]臣超犬馬齒殲，常恐年衰，奄忽僵仆，孤魂□捐。

translations.literal: [50] Your servant Chao's dog-and-horse teeth are worn; he constantly fears advancing years, sudden stiff fall, and a lone soul cast away.
translations.idiomatic: [50] I am old in service as a man's dog grows old; I dread that weakness will strike me down and my ghost wander rootless in the sand.

sentences.zh: 昔蘇武留匈奴中尚十九年，今臣幸得奉節帶金銀護西域，[51]如自以壽終屯部，誠無所恨，然恐後世或名臣為沒西域。

translations.literal: Formerly Su Wu remained among the Xiongnu still nineteen years; now your servant has fortunately been able to bear the staff with gold and silver and protect the Western Regions; [51] if of himself he ends his life in the garrison department, truly there would be nothing to regret, yet he fears later ages may name your servant as one lost in the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: Su Wu endured nineteen years among the Xiongnu; I have been honored with the golden staff as protector of the west. [51] If I die at my post I will not complain, yet I fear later historians will say Ban Chao vanished into the desert.

sentences.zh: 臣不敢望到酒泉郡，但願生入玉門關。

translations.literal: Your servant does not dare hope to reach Jiuquan commandery; he only wishes to enter alive through Jade Gate Pass.
translations.idiomatic: I dare not ask to reach Jiuquan; I beg only to pass through the Jade Gate alive.

sentences.zh: [52]臣老病衰困，冒死瞽言，謹遣子勇隨獻物入塞。

translations.literal: [52] Your servant is old, ill, declining, and exhausted; risking death he speaks blind words; respectfully he sends his son Yong to follow tribute objects in entering the barrier.
translations.idiomatic: [52] Old, ill, and failing, I risk these foolish words and send my son Yong inland with the tribute train.

sentences.zh: [53]及臣生在，令勇目見中土。

translations.literal: [53] While your servant still lives, let Yong with his own eyes see the central land.
translations.idiomatic: [53] While I still breathe, let Yong set eyes on the heartland of Han."

sentences.zh: 」而超妹同郡曹壽妻昭亦上書請超曰：

translations.literal: " Moreover Chao's younger sister, the wife of Cao Shou of the same commandery, Ban Zhao, also submitted a memorial requesting for Chao, saying:
translations.idiomatic: " His sister Ban Zhao, wife of Cao Shou of the same commandery, also laid a memorial on his behalf, saying:

sentences.zh: 妾同產兄西域都護定遠侯超，幸得以微功特蒙重賞，爵列通侯，位二千石。

translations.literal: Your servant's elder brother by the same birth, Ban Chao, protector-general of the Western Regions and marquis of Dingyuan, has fortunately by slight merit specially received heavy reward, his noble rank listed among the full marquises, his position at two thousand piculs.
translations.idiomatic: My elder brother Ban Chao, protector-general of the west and Marquis of Dingyuan, was raised by your grace from small merit to a full marquisate and a two-thousand-picul post.

sentences.zh: 天恩殊絕，誠非小臣所當被蒙。

translations.literal: Heaven's favor is extraordinary beyond measure; truly this is not what a petty minister ought to receive.
translations.idiomatic: Such favor is far beyond what a humble subject may expect.

sentences.zh: 超之始出，志捐軀命，冀立微功，以自陳效。

translations.literal: When Chao first went forth, his intent was to cast away body and life, hoping to establish slight merit so as to display his loyalty.
translations.idiomatic: When he first rode west, he meant to give his life for a modest name on the frontier.

sentences.zh: 會陳睦之變，道路隔絕，超以一身轉側絕域，曉譬諸國，因其兵觿，每有攻戰，輒為先登，身被金夷，[54]不避死亡。

translations.literal: It happened that with Chen Mu's disaster, roads and passes were cut off; Chao with a single body turned about in the cut-off regions, enlightened the various states, and relying on their troop hosts, whenever there was attack or battle, always was first to ascend; his body bore metal wounds, [54] not avoiding death.
translations.idiomatic: Then came Chen Mu's catastrophe and the closing of the roads; alone in the desert he turned kingdom after kingdom to Han, led their hosts in every assault, took the van himself, and bore wound on wound [54] without flinching from death.

sentences.zh: 賴蒙陛下神靈，且得延命沙漠，至今積三十年。

translations.literal: Having relied on receiving your majesty's spiritual power, he has moreover been able to prolong his life in the desert; down to the present it has accumulated thirty years.
translations.idiomatic: By your majesty's blessing he has survived the sands for thirty years.

sentences.zh: 骨肉生離，不復相識。

translations.literal: Flesh and bone have been born apart and no longer know each other.
translations.idiomatic: Kin have been torn apart so long they no longer know one another's faces.

sentences.zh: 所與相隨時人士觿，皆已物故。

translations.literal: The gentlemen and hosts who accompanied him in the same age have all already passed away.
translations.idiomatic: The comrades who marched with him in his prime are all dead.

sentences.zh: 超年最長，今且七十。

translations.literal: Chao is eldest in years; now he is nearly seventy.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao is the oldest of them, nearly seventy now.

sentences.zh: 衰老被病，頭髮無黑，兩手不仁，[55]耳目不聰明，扶杖乃能行。

translations.literal: Declining and worn by sickness, his hair has no black; his two hands lack feeling, [55] ears and eyes are not sharp and clear; leaning on a staff he is only then able to walk.
translations.idiomatic: Age and illness have left him white-haired, numb in both hands, dim of eye and ear; [55] he can walk only with a staff.

sentences.zh: 雖欲竭盡其力，以報塞天恩，迫於歲暮，犬馬齒索。

translations.literal: Although he wishes to exhaust his strength to repay and block up heaven's grace, he is pressed by year's end; his dog-and-horse teeth are worn.
translations.idiomatic: He would still give his last strength for the throne, but the years are against him; his body is spent.

sentences.zh: 蠻夷之性，悖逆侮老，而超旦暮入地，久不見代，恐開奸宄之源，生逆亂之心。

translations.literal: The nature of the Man and Yi is to rebel and insult the old, while Chao morning and evening enters the earth; long without seeing a replacement, I fear this will open the source of treachery and give birth to hearts of rebellion and disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Barbarians scorn the weak and old. Ban Chao stands at death's door, yet no successor is named—I fear that invites intrigue and revolt.

sentences.zh: 而卿大夫咸懷一切，莫肯遠慮。

translations.literal: Yet the high ministers all embrace a one-size-fits-all approach and none is willing to plan far ahead.
translations.idiomatic: Yet the ministers cling to short-term expedients and will not look ahead.

sentences.zh: 如有卒暴，超之氣力不能從心，便為上損國家累世之功，下□忠臣竭力之用，誠可痛也。

translations.literal: If there should be sudden violence, Chao's breath and strength will not follow his heart; then above he would injure the merit accumulated for the state through generations, and below would destroy the loyal minister's use of exhausted strength—truly it is pitiable.
translations.idiomatic: If crisis strikes and his strength fails him, the empire loses the work of generations and a loyal servant loses the use of his last breath—how bitter that would be.

sentences.zh: 故超萬里歸誠，自陳苦急，延頸踰望，三年於今，未蒙省錄。

translations.literal: Therefore Chao, from ten thousand li, returns sincerity and himself sets forth his bitter urgency; stretching his neck he has looked beyond expectation—for three years until now he has not received notice and recording.
translations.idiomatic: For three years he has stretched his neck toward Luoyang, pouring out his plea from the ends of the earth, yet no answer has come.

translations.literal: [56] The transmitted text marks an editorial note here without added wording.
translations.idiomatic: [56] The edition carries only this annotation marker at this point.

sentences.zh: 妾竊聞古者十五受兵，六十還之，[57]亦有休息不任職也。

translations.literal: Your servant has privately heard that in antiquity at fifteen one received weapons and at sixty returned them, [57] and there was also rest from not holding office.
translations.idiomatic: In olden days men took arms at fifteen and laid them down at sixty; [57] even soldiers were given rest.

sentences.zh: 緣陛下以至孝理天下，得萬國之歡心，不遺小國之臣，況超得備侯伯之位，故敢觸死為超求哀，□超餘年。

translations.literal: Following your majesty, who by utmost filial piety orders the realm, has won the joyful hearts of the myriad states and does not cast away ministers of small states—how much more when Chao has obtained preparation among marquis and earl ranks—therefore I dare touch death to beg pity for Chao and beg the remaining years of Chao's life.
translations.idiomatic: You rule all under heaven with supreme filial piety and never scorn the envoys of small states—still less should you abandon a servant already raised to marquis rank. I risk death to beg you to grant my brother whatever years remain to him.

sentences.zh: [58]一得生還，復見闕庭，使國永無勞遠之慮，西域無倉卒之憂，超得長蒙文王葬骨之恩，子方哀老之惠。

translations.literal: [58] Once he can return alive and again see the palace court, it will make the state forever without the worry of toil in the distance and the Western Regions without the anxiety of sudden crisis; Chao will long receive the grace of King Wen who buried bones, and the kindness of Zifang who pitied the old.
translations.idiomatic: [58] Let him live to see the capital again, and the court need never fear another long western campaign, nor the oases dread sudden disaster. He will owe you the kindness King Wen showed to dry bones, the compassion Duke Zifang showed the aged.

sentences.zh: [59]詩云：「民亦勞止，汔可小康，惠此中國，以綏四方。

translations.literal: [59] The Odes says: 'The people also are weary; may they have a little rest; show favor to this central land and thereby pacify the four quarters.'
translations.idiomatic: [59] The Classic says: 'The people are weary—grant them a little peace; cherish the heartland and the four quarters will be still.'

sentences.zh: 」[60]超有書與妾生訣，恐不復相見。

translations.literal: " [60] Chao has written to your servant a living farewell, fearing they will not again see each other.
translations.idiomatic: [60] He has written me what may be our last farewell.

sentences.zh: 妾誠傷超以壯年竭忠孝於沙漠，疲老則便捐死於曠野，誠可哀憐。

translations.literal: Your servant truly grieves that Chao in the prime of life exhausted loyalty and filial piety in the desert, and when weary and old will then be cast aside to die in the open wastes—truly pitiable and to be pitied.
translations.idiomatic: It breaks my heart that he gave his youth and loyalty to the sands and may be left to die in the wilderness in old age.

sentences.zh: 如不蒙救護，超後有一旦之變，冀幸超家得蒙趙母、□姬先請之貸。

translations.literal: If he is not rescued and protected, and after Chao there should be a morning's change, I hope and pray that Chao's family may receive the indulgence of Zhao's mother and Wei Ji's prior pleas.
translations.idiomatic: If he is not spared and some morning brings disaster, I pray only that his family may win the same mercy Zhao's mother and Lady Wei once obtained for their kin.

sentences.zh: [61]妾愚戇不知大義，觸犯忌諱。

translations.literal: [61] Your servant is foolish and blunt and does not know great righteousness; she offends against taboo.
translations.idiomatic: [61] I am a foolish woman who may have overstepped—but I speak from the heart."

sentences.zh: 書奏，帝感其言，乃徵超還。

translations.literal: When the memorial was presented, the emperor was moved by her words and thereupon summoned Chao to return.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was moved and recalled Ban Chao to the capital.

sentences.zh: 超在西域三十一歲。

translations.literal: Chao was in the Western Regions thirty-one years.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao spent thirty-one years in the Western Regions.

sentences.zh: 十四年八月至洛陽，拜為射聲校尉。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year, the eighth month, he reached Luoyang and was appointed colonel of the sound-shooters.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month of Yongyuan 14 (102 CE) he reached Luoyang and was named colonel of the sound-shooters.

sentences.zh: 超素有匈脅疾，既至，病遂加。

translations.literal: Chao had long had an ailment in the chest and ribs; when he arrived, the sickness thereupon worsened.
translations.idiomatic: He had long suffered chest pain; the journey made it worse.

sentences.zh: 帝遣中黃門問疾，賜醫藥。

translations.literal: The emperor sent a palace eunuch to inquire after his illness and bestowed physicians and drugs.
translations.idiomatic: The court sent a eunuch physician with imperial medicines.

sentences.zh: 其年九月卒，年七十一。

translations.literal: That year in the ninth month he died, aged seventy-one.
translations.idiomatic: He died that September at seventy-one.

sentences.zh: 朝廷愍惜焉，使者弔祭，贈賵甚厚。

translations.literal: The court pitied and regretted him; envoys offered condolences and sacrifices, and the funeral gifts were very generous.
translations.idiomatic: The court mourned him deeply: envoys performed the rites of condolence and the burial gifts were lavish.

sentences.zh: 子雄嗣。

translations.literal: His son Xiong succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Ban Xiong inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 初，超被徵，以戊己校尉任尚為都護。

translations.literal: Earlier, when Chao was summoned, Ren Shang, colonel of the Wu and Ji, was made protector-general.
translations.idiomatic: When Ban Chao was recalled, Ren Shang of the Wu-Ji colonelcy was named protector-general.

sentences.zh: 與超交代。

translations.literal: He handed over with Chao.
translations.idiomatic: He took over from Ban Chao.

sentences.zh: 尚謂超曰：「君侯在外國三十餘年，而小人猥承君後，任重慮淺，宜有以誨之。

translations.literal: Shang said to Chao, "For more than thirty years, lord, you have been in foreign lands, while this petty man basely succeeds after you; the burden is heavy and my forethought shallow—you ought to have something to instruct me."
translations.idiomatic: Ren Shang said, "You spent thirty years abroad; I am a lesser man following in your tracks, heavy duty and shallow judgment—give me your counsel."

sentences.zh: 」超曰：「年老失智，任君數當大位，豈班超所能及哉！

translations.literal: " Chao said, "Old in years, I have lost wisdom; you, sir, have several times held great position—how could Ban Chao reach that!"
translations.idiomatic: " Ban Chao answered, "I am old and dull; you have risen to high office again and again—what could I teach you?"

sentences.zh: 必不得已，願進愚言。

translations.literal: " If it cannot be avoided, I wish to advance foolish words.
translations.idiomatic: " If you insist, I offer a few plain words."

sentences.zh: 塞外吏士，本非孝子順孫，皆以罪過徙補邊屯。

translations.literal: The clerks and soldiers beyond the barrier were originally not filial sons and obedient grandsons; all were moved to patch border garrisons because of crimes and faults.
translations.idiomatic: The garrison troops beyond the pass are not dutiful sons; most were exiled thither for crime.

sentences.zh: 而蠻夷懷鳥獸之心，難養易敗。

translations.literal: Moreover the Man and Yi harbor the hearts of birds and beasts—hard to nurture, easy to ruin.
translations.idiomatic: The frontier peoples are like wild things: hard to tame, quick to turn.

sentences.zh: 今君性嚴急，水清無大魚，察政不得下和。

translations.literal: Now your nature is strict and hasty; when the water is too clear there are no great fish, and scrutinizing government cannot obtain harmony below.
translations.idiomatic: You are stern and impatient. Clear water holds no big fish; pick at every fault and you lose the men's goodwill.

sentences.zh: [62]宜蕩佚簡易，寬小過，總大綱而已。

translations.literal: [62] It is fitting to be expansive and easy, lenient with small faults, grasping only the great outlines—that is all.
