translations.idiomatic: [62] Be easy and broad, forgive small slips, hold only the main lines—that is enough."

sentences.zh: 」超去後，尚私謂所親曰：「我以班君當有奇策，今所言平平耳。

translations.literal: " After Chao departed, Shang privately said to those close to him, "I thought Lord Ban would have extraordinary stratagems; what he said was merely ordinary."
translations.idiomatic: " After Ban Chao left, Ren Shang muttered to friends, "I expected marvels; all I got was platitudes."

sentences.zh: 」尚至數年，而西域反亂，以罪被征，如超所戒。

translations.literal: " After Shang arrived, within several years the Western Regions rebelled and were in disorder; because of crime he was summoned—just as Chao had warned.
translations.idiomatic: " Within a few years the west rose in revolt; Ren Shang was recalled in disgrace—exactly as Ban Chao had foretold."

sentences.zh: 有三子。

translations.literal: He had three sons.
translations.idiomatic: He had three sons.

sentences.zh: 長子雄，累遷屯騎校尉。

translations.literal: The eldest son, Xiong, was repeatedly promoted to colonel of the garrison cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: The eldest, Ban Xiong, rose to colonel of the garrison cavalry.

sentences.zh: 會叛羌寇三輔，詔雄將五營兵屯長安，就拜京兆尹。

translations.literal: It happened that rebel Qiang raided the three adjuncts; an edict ordered Xiong to lead the five-camp troops and garrison Chang'an; he was then appointed metropolitan governor of Jingzhao.
translations.idiomatic: When rebel Qiang threatened the capital region, Ban Xiong was ordered to lead the five camps to Chang'an and was appointed metropolitan governor of Jingzhao.

sentences.zh: 雄卒，子始嗣，尚清河孝王女陰城公主。

translations.literal: When Xiong died, his son Shi succeeded and married the Princess of Yincheng, daughter of Prince Xiao of Qinghe.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Xiong died; his son Ban Shi inherited the title and married the Princess of Yincheng, daughter of Prince Xiao of Qinghe.

sentences.zh: 主順帝之姑，貴驕淫亂，與嬖人居帷中，而召始入，使伏默下。

translations.literal: The princess was Emperor Shun's paternal aunt; arrogant and wanton, with a favorite she dwelt within curtains and summoned Shi to enter, making him crouch silent beneath the bed.
translations.idiomatic: She was Emperor Shun's aunt by marriage—proud, dissolute. She hid with a lover behind her curtains, called Ban Shi in, and made him kneel in silence beneath the bed.

sentences.zh: 始積怒，永建五年，遂拔刃殺主。

translations.literal: Shi accumulated rage; in the fifth year of Yongjian, he thereupon drew a blade and killed the princess.
translations.idiomatic: The humiliation festered until in Yongjian 5 Ban Shi drew his sword and killed the princess.

sentences.zh: 帝大怒，腰斬始，同產皆□巿。

translations.literal: The emperor was greatly angry and had Shi cut at the waist; those born of the same womb all were executed at the market.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor in fury had him cut in two at the waist; his full siblings were executed in the marketplace.

sentences.zh: 超少子勇。

translations.literal: Chao's youngest son was Yong.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao's youngest son was Ban Yong.

sentences.zh: 勇字宜僚，少有父風。

translations.literal: Yong, courtesy name Yiliao, in youth had his father's manner.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Yong, style Yiliao, showed his father's mettle even as a youth.

sentences.zh: 永初元年，西域反叛，以勇為軍司馬。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongchu, the Western Regions rebelled; Yong was made army major.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 1 (107 CE), when the west revolted, he was named army major.

sentences.zh: 與兄雄俱出敦煌，迎都護及西域甲卒而還。

translations.literal: Together with his elder brother Xiong he went out from Dunhuang to welcome the protector-general and the armored soldiers of the Western Regions and returned.
translations.idiomatic: He rode out of Dunhuang with Ban Xiong to escort the protector-general and the western garrisons home.

sentences.zh: 因罷都護。

translations.literal: Thereupon the protector-general was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The protector-generalship was then abolished.

sentences.zh: 後西域絕無漢吏十餘年。

translations.literal: Afterward the Western Regions for more than ten years had absolutely no Han officials.
translations.idiomatic: For more than a decade thereafter no Han official set foot in the Tarim basin.

sentences.zh: 元初六年，敦煌太守曹宗遣長史索班將千餘人屯伊吾，車師前王及鄯善王皆來降班。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of Yuanchu, Cao Zong, governor of Dunhuang, sent chief clerk Suo Ban to lead more than a thousand men and garrison Yiwu; the former king of Cheshi forward and the king of Shanshan both came to submit to Ban.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanchu 6 (119 CE) Governor Cao Zong of Dunhuang sent Chief Clerk Suo Ban with a thousand men to hold Yiwu; the king of Forward Cheshi and the king of Shanshan both submitted to him.

sentences.zh: 後數月，北單于與車師後部遂共攻沒班，進擊走前王，略有北道。

translations.literal: After several months, the northern chanyu together with the rear division of Cheshi jointly attacked and destroyed Ban, advanced and drove off the forward king, and seized and held the northern route.
translations.idiomatic: Months later the northern chanyu and Rear Cheshi destroyed Suo Ban, drove out the forward king, and overran the northern track.

sentences.zh: 鄯善王急，求救於曹宗，宗因此請出兵五千人擊匈奴，報索班之恥，因復取西域。

translations.literal: The king of Shanshan was urgent and begged rescue from Cao Zong; Zong on this account requested to dispatch five thousand troops to strike the Xiongnu, avenge the shame of Suo Ban, and thereby recover the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Shanshan begged Cao Zong for aid; Cao asked leave to send five thousand men against the Xiongnu to avenge Suo Ban and reopen the west.

sentences.zh: 鄧太后召勇詣朝堂會議。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Deng summoned Yong to the court hall for deliberation.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Deng summoned Ban Yong to court for debate.

sentences.zh: 先是公卿多以為宜閉玉門關，遂□西域。

translations.literal: Earlier the dukes and ministers for the most part had held that it was fitting to close Jade Gate Pass and thereby abandon the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: Most ministers had already argued for sealing the Jade Gate and abandoning the west.

sentences.zh: 勇上議曰：「昔孝武皇帝患匈奴強盛，兼總百蠻，以逼障塞。

translations.literal: Yong submitted his opinion, saying, "Formerly Emperor Xiaowu grieved that the Xiongnu were strong and prosperous and gathered the hundred barbarians to press the barrier passes.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Yong argued: "Emperor Wu faced a powerful Xiongnu confederation choking the frontier."

sentences.zh: 於是開通西域，離其黨與，論者以為奪匈奴府藏，斷其右臂。

translations.literal: Thereupon he opened communication with the Western Regions, separated their party and allies; debaters thought this seized the Xiongnu treasury and cut off their right arm.
translations.idiomatic: He opened the Western Regions to split their allies—strategists called it emptying the Xiongnu treasury and severing their right arm."

sentences.zh: 遭王莽篡盜，徵求無猒，胡夷忿毒，遂以背叛。

translations.literal: They encountered Wang Mang's usurpation and plunder; requisitions were without satiety; the Hu and Yi resented poisonously and thereupon rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang's exactions turned them against us; the tribes burned with hatred and rose in revolt.

sentences.zh: 光武中興，未遑外事，故匈奴負強，驅率諸國。

translations.literal: At Guangwu's restoration, there was no leisure for outer affairs; therefore the Xiongnu relied on strength and drove and led the various states.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu had no leisure for the frontier, so the Xiongnu bullied the oases at will.

sentences.zh: 及至永平，再攻敦煌，河西諸郡，城門晝閉。

translations.literal: Down to Yongping they twice attacked Dunhuang; the commanderies of Hexi closed their city gates by day.
translations.idiomatic: By Yongping they were raiding Dunhuang twice over; Hexi barred its gates even in daylight.

sentences.zh: 孝明皇帝深惟廟策，[63]乃命虎臣，出征西域，[64]故匈奴遠遁，邊境得安。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaoming deeply pondered the temple's strategy, [63] and thereupon ordered tiger ministers to go out on campaign to the Western Regions; [64] therefore the Xiongnu fled far and the border regions obtained peace.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming weighed the ancestral counsel [63] and sent his generals west; [64] the Xiongnu fled and the border knew peace again.

sentences.zh: 及至永元，莫不內屬。

translations.literal: Down to Yongyuan none failed to submit inwardly.
translations.idiomatic: By Yongyuan every kingdom had submitted.

sentences.zh: 會閒者羌亂，西域復絕，北虜遂遣責諸國，備其逋租，高其價直，嚴以期會。

translations.literal: It happened that recently there was Qiang disorder; the Western Regions again were cut off; the northern barbarians thereupon sent to press the various states, equip their defaulted rent, raise their price value, and strictly enforce deadlines.
translations.idiomatic: Then Qiang troubles cut the road again; the northern chanyu extorted the oases for back tribute at ruinous rates on pain of war.

sentences.zh: 鄯善、車師皆懷憤怨，思樂事漢，其路無從。

translations.literal: Shanshan and Cheshi both harbor indignant resentment and long to gladly serve Han, but their road has no outlet.
translations.idiomatic: Shanshan and Cheshi burn with resentment yet long to serve Han—they have no opening.

sentences.zh: 前所以時有叛者，皆由牧養失宜，還為其害故也。

translations.literal: The reason there were from time to time rebels earlier was all because shepherding and nurturing were improper, and they returned to harm them.
translations.idiomatic: Their earlier revolts came from bad policy, not from innate treachery.

sentences.zh: 今曹宗徒恥於前負，欲報雪匈奴，而不尋出兵故事，未度當時之宜也。

translations.literal: Now Cao Zong is ashamed only of the former defeat and wishes to avenge himself on the Xiongnu, yet does not seek the precedents of dispatching troops and has not measured what suits the present moment.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Zong smart from defeat wants revenge without studying how campaigns were run or what the moment allows.

sentences.zh: 夫要功荒外，萬無一成，若兵連禍結，悔無及已。

translations.literal: To seek merit in the wild beyond is ten thousand times without one success; if arms drag on and disaster knots, regret will not catch up.
translations.idiomatic: Adventures beyond the wall almost never pay; if war drags on, remorse will come too late.

sentences.zh: 況今府藏未充，師無後繼，是示弱於遠夷，暴短於海內，臣愚以為不可許也。

translations.literal: Moreover now the treasuries are not full and the army has no follow-on force—this shows weakness to distant Yi and exposes shortcoming within the seas; your servant foolishly thinks it cannot be granted.
translations.idiomatic: The treasury is thin and there is no second army—this would advertise weakness to the tribes and fault at home. I say the proposal should be refused.

sentences.zh: 舊敦煌郡有營兵三百人，今宜復之，復置護西域副校尉，居於敦煌，如永元故事。

translations.literal: Formerly Dunhuang commandery had garrison troops three hundred men; now it is fitting to restore them and again install the deputy colonel for the Western Regions, dwelling at Dunhuang, according to the precedent of Yongyuan.
translations.idiomatic: Restore Dunhuang's three hundred garrison troops and revive the deputy colonel for the west at Dunhuang, as under Yongyuan.

sentences.zh: 又宜遣西域長史將五百人屯樓蘭，西當焉耆、龜茲徑路，南強鄯善、于窴心膽，北扞匈奴，東近敦煌。

translations.literal: It is also fitting to send the chief clerk of the Western Regions to lead five hundred men and garrison Loulan, blocking on the west the direct routes of Yanqi and Qiuci, strengthening on the south the hearts and gall of Shanshan and Yutian, defending on the north against the Xiongnu, and drawing near on the east to Dunhuang.
translations.idiomatic: Send the western chief clerk with five hundred men to Loulan—blocking Yanqi and Kucha to the west, heartening Shanshan and Khotan to the south, shielding against the Xiongnu to the north, within reach of Dunhuang to the east.

sentences.zh: 如此誠便。」

translations.literal: If it is thus, truly it is convenient."
translations.idiomatic: That is the prudent course."

sentences.zh: 尚書問勇曰：「今立副校尉，何以為便？

translations.literal: The secretariat asked Yong, "Now in establishing the deputy colonel, wherein lies the convenience?"
translations.idiomatic: The ministers asked: "What good comes of reviving the deputy colonel?"

sentences.zh: 又置長史屯樓蘭，利害云何？

translations.literal: Moreover in placing the chief clerk to garrison Loulan, what of benefit and harm?"
translations.idiomatic: And what of posting the chief clerk at Loulan?"

sentences.zh: 」勇對曰：「昔永平之末，始通西域，初遣中郎將居敦煌，後置副校*[尉]*於車師，既為胡虜節度，又禁漢人不得有所侵擾。

translations.literal: " Yong replied, "At the end of Yongping, when communication with the Western Regions first opened, at first a gentleman of the interior was stationed at Dunhuang; afterward the deputy colonel was placed at Cheshi; he both regulated the Hu barbarians and forbade Han people from committing encroachment or harassment.
translations.idiomatic: " Ban Yong answered: "At the end of Yongping, when the road first opened, a court officer held Dunhuang and a deputy colonel sat at Cheshi—controlling the tribes while barring Han from abuses."

sentences.zh: 故外夷歸心，匈奴畏威。

translations.literal: Therefore outer Yi submitted in heart and the Xiongnu feared might.
translations.idiomatic: The tribes trusted us and the Xiongnu feared our shadow.

sentences.zh: 今鄯善王尤還，[65]漢人外孫，若匈奴得志，則尤還必死。

translations.literal: Now the king of Shanshan, You Huan, [65] is a Han person's maternal grandson; if the Xiongnu obtain their will, then You Huan will certainly die.
translations.idiomatic: King You Huan of Shanshan is a Han grandson by the female line; [65] if the Xiongnu have their way, he is a dead man.

sentences.zh: 此等雖同鳥獸，亦知避害。

translations.literal: Though these are the same as birds and beasts, they also know to avoid harm.
translations.idiomatic: Even brutes flee danger.

sentences.zh: 若出屯樓蘭，足以招附其心，愚以為便。

translations.literal: If we go out and garrison Loulan, it suffices to summon and attach their hearts; in foolish opinion this is convenient."
translations.idiomatic: A garrison at Loulan would win their allegiance again. That is my counsel."

sentences.zh: 」長樂□尉鐔顯、廷尉綦母參、司隸校尉崔據難曰：

translations.literal: " Colonel of Changle Tan Xian, commandant of the court Qimu Can, and metropolitan commandant Cui Ju objected, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Tan Xian, colonel of the Changle guard, Qimu Can the commandant of the court, and Cui Ju the metropolitan commandant objected:

sentences.zh: 「朝廷前所以□西域者，以其無益於中國而費難供也。

translations.literal: " The court formerly abandoned the Western Regions because they were of no benefit to the central states and supplies were hard to furnish.
translations.idiomatic: " The court gave up the west because it drained the interior without return."

sentences.zh: 今車師已屬匈奴，鄯善不可保信，一旦反覆，班將能保北虜不為邊害乎？

translations.literal: Now Cheshi already belongs to the Xiongnu; Shanshan cannot be trusted; once they turn back, can Colonel Ban guarantee the northern barbarians will not become a border harm?"
translations.idiomatic: Cheshi is now Xiongnu territory; Shanshan cannot be trusted. If they turn again, can Ban Yong swear the tribes will not threaten the border?"

sentences.zh: 」[66]勇對曰：「今中國置州牧者，以禁郡縣奸猾盜賊也。

translations.literal: " [66] Yong replied, "Now in the central states we install regional shepherds to forbid crafty villainy and banditry in commanderies and counties.
translations.idiomatic: " [66] Ban Yong answered: "We set provincial shepherds to curb thieves in the commanderies."

sentences.zh: 若州牧能保盜賊不起者，臣亦願以要斬保匈奴之不為邊害也。

translations.literal: If regional shepherds can guarantee bandits will not arise, your servant likewise wishes to guarantee by beheading at the waist that the Xiongnu will not become a border harm.
translations.idiomatic: If a shepherd could swear no thief would ever rise, I would pledge my neck that the Xiongnu would not trouble the passes."

sentences.zh: 今通西域則虜埶必弱，虜埶*

translations.literal: Now if we open communication with the Western Regions then the barbarian situation must weaken; the barbarian situation *
translations.idiomatic: Reopening the west weakens the Xiongnu; a weaker foe *

translations.literal: (The transmitted text inserts a collation mark here.)
translations.idiomatic: (The edition carries a collation mark in this sentence.)

sentences.zh: *弱則為患微矣。

translations.literal: * if weak then the harm they cause is slight.
translations.idiomatic: * means slighter danger on the frontier.

sentences.zh: 孰與歸其府藏，續其斷臂哉！

translations.literal: How does that compare to returning their treasury stores and reconnecting their severed arm!"
translations.idiomatic: How does that compare to handing them back their granaries and sewing on their severed arm?"

sentences.zh: 今置校尉以扞撫西域，設長史以招懷諸國，若□而不立，則西域望絕。

translations.literal: Now if we install a colonel to guard and soothe the Western Regions and set a chief clerk to summon and cherish the various states, if we cut off and do not establish these, then the Western Regions' hope will be severed.
translations.idiomatic: A colonel reassures the oases; a chief clerk woos their kings. Abandon both and the west gives up hope.

sentences.zh: 望絕之後，屈就北虜，緣邊之郡將受困害，恐河西城門必復有晝閉之儆矣。

translations.literal: After hope is severed, they will bend to the northern barbarians; the commanderies along the border will receive harm and distress, and I fear the gates of Hexi will again have the warning of being closed by day.
translations.idiomatic: Despair drives them into the Xiongnu orbit, and the border commanderies will suffer—I fear Hexi will bar its gates in daylight once more.

sentences.zh: 今不廓開朝廷之德，而拘屯戍之費，若北虜遂熾，豈安邊久長之策哉！」

translations.literal: Now if we do not enlarge and open the court's virtue but cling to the expense of garrisoning—if the northern barbarians thereupon blaze bright, is that a policy for long-lasting border peace!"
translations.idiomatic: To stint on a little garrison silver while the northern power swells is no way to keep the frontier quiet for long!"

sentences.zh: 太尉屬毛軫難曰：「今若置校尉，則西域駱驛遣使，求索無猒，與之則費難供，不與則失其心。

translations.literal: Mao Zhen, a subordinate of the grand commandant, objected, saying, "Now if we install a colonel, then the Western Regions will send envoys in relays, seeking and begging without satiety; if we give to them supplies are hard to furnish, if we do not give we lose their hearts.
translations.idiomatic: Mao Zhen of the grand commandant's staff objected: "A colonel means endless embassies from the west, insatiable demands—pay and you bankrupt the treasury; refuse and you lose them."

sentences.zh: 一旦為匈奴所迫，當復求救，則為役大矣。

translations.literal: Once they are pressed by the Xiongnu, they will again beg rescue—then the corvée will be great."
translations.idiomatic: When the Xiongnu lean on them again they will beg for armies—that is the heavier burden."

sentences.zh: 」勇對曰：「今設以西域歸匈奴，而使其恩德大漢，不為鈔盜則可矣。

translations.literal: " Yong replied, "Suppose we grant the Western Regions to the Xiongnu and thereby make them in great grace with Han and not raiding or robbing—that could be.
translations.idiomatic: " Ban Yong replied: "If yielding the west made the Xiongnu grateful and ended their raids, fine."

sentences.zh: 如其不然，則因西域租入之饒，兵馬之觿，以擾動緣邊，是為富仇讎之財，增暴夷之埶也。

translations.literal: If it is not so, then relying on the Western Regions' abundance of rent income and multitude of troops and horses, they will use them to disturb and move the border regions—this is to enrich an enemy's wealth and increase violent Yi's power.
translations.idiomatic: Otherwise they will tax the oases' wealth and ride their cavalry to harry our border—that feeds our foe and swells a savage power.

sentences.zh: 置校尉者，宣威布德，以系諸國內向之心，以疑匈奴覬覦之情，而無財費耗國之慮也。

translations.literal: To install a colonel is to spread might and extend virtue, to tie the various states' hearts of turning inward, to cast doubt on the Xiongnu's covetous disposition, and to have no worry of treasure expense exhausting the state.
translations.idiomatic: A colonel projects Han power and virtue, keeps the kingdoms facing inward, checks Xiongnu ambition, and costs the treasury far less than a full war."

sentences.zh: 且西域之人無它求索，其來入者，不過稟食而已。

translations.literal: Moreover people of the Western Regions have no other requests or searches; those who come in seek nothing beyond receiving grain rations.
translations.idiomatic: The westerners are not greedy—when they visit they mainly want their government grain.

sentences.zh: 今若拒絕，埶歸北屬，夷虜並力以寇並、涼，則中國之費不止千億。

translations.literal: If now we reject and cut them off, the situation turns to submission in the north; barbarians and invaders join strength to raid Bing and Liang, and then the central states' expense will not stop at a thousand million.
translations.idiomatic: Shut them out and they swing to the Xiongnu; united steppe power will ravage Bing and Liang, and the interior will pay far more than a few garrison piculs.

sentences.zh: 置之誠便。」

translations.literal: To install these is truly the convenient course."
translations.idiomatic: Adopt the plan—it is plainly the cheaper course."

sentences.zh: 於是從勇議，復敦煌郡營兵三百人，置西域副校尉居敦煌。

translations.literal: Thereupon following Yong's proposal, they restored three hundred garrison troops of Dunhuang commandery and installed the deputy colonel for the Western Regions dwelling at Dunhuang.
translations.idiomatic: The court followed Ban Yong: three hundred Dunhuang garrison troops and a deputy colonel for the west based at Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 雖復羈縻西域，然亦未能出屯。

translations.literal: Although again they loosely tied the Western Regions, yet they also were not able to go out and establish forward garrisons.
translations.idiomatic: The west was held only by loose suzerainty; the forward posts Yong had urged were never planted.

sentences.zh: 其後匈奴果數與車師共入寇鈔，河西大被其害。

translations.literal: Afterward the Xiongnu indeed several times together with Cheshi entered to raid and plunder; Hexi greatly suffered their harm.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu then raided Hexi again and again with Cheshi, and the northwest paid dearly.

sentences.zh: 延光二年夏，復以勇為西域長史，將兵五百人出屯柳中。

translations.literal: In the summer of the second year of Yanguang, Yong was again appointed chief clerk of the Western Regions, leading five hundred troops to go out and garrison Liuzhong.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanguang 2 summer Ban Yong was again named western chief clerk and led five hundred men to hold Liuzhong.

sentences.zh: [67]明年正月，勇至樓蘭，以鄯善歸附，特加三綬。

translations.literal: [67] In the first month of the next year, when Yong reached Loulan, because Shanshan had returned and submitted, he specially added three seal-ribbons.
translations.idiomatic: [67] The next first month he reached Loulan and, because Shanshan had renewed fealty, upgraded their seals with three added ribbons.

sentences.zh: 而龜茲王白英猶自疑未下，勇開以恩信，白英乃率姑墨、溫宿自縛詣勇降。

translations.literal: But King Bai Ying of Qiuci still doubted and had not submitted; Yong opened him with grace and good faith, and Bai Ying thereupon led Gumo and Wensu, binding themselves, to come to Yong and submit.
translations.idiomatic: King Bai Ying of Kucha still wavered until Ban Yong won him with trust; then Bai Ying came in bonds with the kings of Gumo and Wensu to surrender.

sentences.zh: 勇因發其兵步騎萬餘人到車師前王庭，擊走匈奴伊蠡王於伊和谷，收得前部五千餘人，於是前部始復開通。

translations.literal: Yong thereupon mobilized their troops, more than ten thousand infantry and cavalry, to the court of the forward king of Cheshi, struck and drove off the Xiongnu King Yili in the Yihe valley, collected more than five thousand men of the forward division, and thereupon the forward division first was reopened.
translations.idiomatic: He marched their ten thousand-odd horse and foot to Forward Cheshi's royal camp, routed the Xiongnu Yili king in the Yihe gorge, rallied over five thousand men of the forward division, and reopened the northern route.

sentences.zh: 還，屯田柳中。

translations.literal: Returning, he garrison-farmed at Liuzhong.
translations.idiomatic: He then farmed the garrison fields at Liuzhong.

sentences.zh: 四年秋，勇發敦煌、張掖、酒泉六千騎及鄯善、疏勒、車師前部兵擊後部王軍就，大破之。

translations.literal: In the autumn of the fourth year, Yong mobilized six thousand horse from Dunhuang, Zhangye, and Jiuquan and troops of Shanshan, Shule, and the forward division of Cheshi to strike the rear-division king Jungjiu and greatly defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanguang 4 autumn he led six thousand Hexi cavalry plus Shanshan, Shule, and Forward Cheshi against Rear Cheshi's king Jungjiu and shattered his army.

sentences.zh: [68]首虜八千餘人，馬畜五萬餘頭。

translations.literal: [68] Heads and captives were more than eight thousand; horses and livestock more than fifty thousand head.
translations.idiomatic: [68] The butcher's bill passed eight thousand; horses and herds topped fifty thousand head.

sentences.zh: 捕得軍就及匈奴持節使者，將至索班沒處斬之，以報其恥，傳首京師。

translations.literal: They captured Jungjiu and the Xiongnu envoy bearing the staff; they brought them to the place where Suo Ban perished and beheaded them, to avenge that shame, and sent the heads to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Jungjiu and the Xiongnu credential-bearer were executed on the ground where Suo Ban fell, avenging his death; their heads were sent to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 永建元年，更立後部故王子加特奴為王。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongjian, they replaced the king by establishing Jianu, a former prince of the rear division, as king.
translations.idiomatic: Yongjian 1: Jianu, a prince of Rear Cheshi, was enthroned as king.

sentences.zh: 勇又使別校誅斬東且彌王，亦更立其種人為王，[69]於是車師六國悉平。

translations.literal: Yong again sent a separate column to execute and behead the king of Eastern Jumi and also replaced him with a man of their stock as king, [69] thereupon the six states of Cheshi were all pacified.
translations.idiomatic: A detached force killed the king of Eastern Jumi and set a clansman on his throne [69]; the six Cheshi polities were quiet.

sentences.zh: 其冬，勇發諸國兵擊匈奴呼衍王，呼衍王亡走，其觿二萬餘人皆降。

translations.literal: That winter Yong mobilized troops of the various states to strike the Xiongnu Prince Huyan; the prince fled, and more than twenty thousand of his host all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: That winter allied troops struck the Xiongnu Huyan prince; he fled and over twenty thousand of his men yielded.

sentences.zh: 捕得單于從兄，勇使加特奴手斬之，以結車師匈奴之隙。

translations.literal: They captured the chanyu's cousin by the elder branch; Yong ordered Jianu personally to behead him, to knot enmity between Cheshi and the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: They took the chanyu's cousin; Ban Yong had King Jianu cut off his head to set Cheshi and the Xiongnu at odds.

sentences.zh: 北單于自將萬餘騎入後部，至金且谷，勇使假司馬曹俊馳救之。

translations.literal: The northern chanyu himself led more than ten thousand horse into the rear division as far as Jinqiu valley; Yong sent acting major Cao Jun galloping to rescue.
translations.idiomatic: The northern chanyu led ten thousand riders into Rear Cheshi as far as Jinqiu; Ban Yong sent brevet major Cao Jun to relieve the town.

sentences.zh: 單于引去，俊追斬其貴人骨都侯，於是呼衍王遂徙居枯梧河上。

translations.literal: The chanyu drew off; Jun pursued and beheaded their noble Guduhou, and thereupon Prince Huyan thereupon moved to dwell on the Kuwu river.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu withdrew; Cao Jun ran down the noble Guduhou, and Huyan shifted his camp to the Kuwu River.

sentences.zh: 是後車師無復虜跡，城郭皆安。

translations.literal: After this Cheshi had no further traces of barbarians; the walled cities all were secure.
translations.idiomatic: Cheshi saw no more Xiongnu; the oasis towns were safe.

sentences.zh: 唯焉耆王元孟未降。

translations.literal: Only King Yuan Meng of Yanqi had not yet submitted.
translations.idiomatic: Only Yuan Meng of Yanqi still held out.

sentences.zh: 二年，勇上請攻元孟，於是遣敦煌太守張朗將河西四郡兵三千人配勇。

translations.literal: In the second year Yong submitted a request to attack Yuan Meng; thereupon Dunhuang governor Zhang Lang was dispatched to lead three thousand troops of the four Hexi commanderies assigned to Yong.
translations.idiomatic: Yongjian 2: Ban Yong asked to attack Yuan Meng; Zhang Lang, governor of Dunhuang, was given three thousand men from four Hexi commanderies to join him.

sentences.zh: [70]因發諸國兵四萬餘人，分騎為兩道擊之。

translations.literal: [70] He thereupon mobilized troops of the various states, more than forty thousand men, divided cavalry into two routes and struck them.
translations.idiomatic: [70] He raised over forty thousand allied troops and split them into two columns.

sentences.zh: 勇從南道，朗從北道，約期俱至焉耆。

translations.literal: Yong took the southern route, Lang the northern route, agreeing on a date both to reach Yanqi.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Yong took the southern road, Zhang Lang the northern, with a rendezvous at Yanqi.

sentences.zh: 而朗先有罪，欲徼功自贖，遂先期至爵離關，遣司馬將兵前戰，首虜二千餘人。

translations.literal: But Lang earlier had a crime and wished to seize merit to redeem himself; he therefore arrived ahead of time at Jueliguan, sent a major with troops to fight in advance, and took more than two thousand heads and captives.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Lang, under a cloud, raced ahead to win pardon: he reached Jueliguan early, sent a major forward, and claimed over two thousand heads.

sentences.zh: 元孟懼誅，逆遣使乞降，張朗徑入焉耆受降而還。

translations.literal: Yuan Meng feared execution and in advance sent envoys begging to surrender; Zhang Lang went straight into Yanqi, received the surrender, and returned.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Meng, fearing death, sued for peace; Zhang Lang marched straight into Yanqi, accepted the surrender, and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 元孟竟不肯面縛，唯遣子詣闕貢獻。

translations.literal: Yuan Meng in the end was unwilling to come bound with hands behind; he only sent his son to the palace gate to present tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Meng refused to appear with corded hands—he sent only his son to Luoyang with tribute.

sentences.zh: 朗遂得免誅。

translations.literal: Lang thereupon obtained exemption from execution.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Lang escaped punishment.

sentences.zh: 勇以後期，徵下獄，免。

translations.literal: Yong because he was late on the schedule was summoned and cast into prison and dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Yong, late to the rendezvous, was jailed and stripped of office.

sentences.zh: 後卒於家。

translations.literal: Later he died at home.
translations.idiomatic: He died at home in obscurity.

sentences.zh: 梁慬字伯威，[71]北地弋居人也。

translations.literal: Liang Jin, courtesy name Bowei, [71] was a man of Yiju in Beidi.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Jin, style Bowei, [71] came from Yiju in Beidi commandery.

sentences.zh: [72]父諷，歷州宰。

translations.literal: [72] His father Feng had served as prefect of provinces.
translations.idiomatic: [72] His father Liang Feng had been a provincial governor.

sentences.zh: 永元元年，車騎將軍竇憲出征匈奴，除諷為軍司馬，令先繼金帛使北單于，宣國威德，其歸附者萬餘人。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongyuan, General-in-chief Dou Xian went out on campaign against the Xiongnu; Feng was appointed army major and ordered first to carry gold and silks to the northern chanyu and proclaim the state's might and virtue; those who submitted numbered more than ten thousand.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 1 (89 CE) Dou Xian marched against the Xiongnu; Liang Feng was named army major and sent ahead with gold and silk to the northern chanyu to proclaim Han power; over ten thousand submitted.

sentences.zh: 後坐失憲意，髡輸武威，武威太守承旨殺之。

translations.literal: Later because he missed Dou Xian's intent, he was shaved and transported to Wuwei; the governor of Wuwei, carrying out secret orders, killed him.
translations.idiomatic: He later offended Dou Xian, was shorn and exiled to Wuwei, and the governor there killed him on orders.

sentences.zh: 竇氏既滅，和帝知其為憲所誣，徵慬，除為郎中。

translations.literal: After the Dou clan was destroyed, the He Emperor knew he had been slandered by Xian and summoned Jin, appointing him gentleman of the interior.
translations.idiomatic: When the Dou fell, Emperor He knew Liang Feng had been framed and summoned Liang Jin as a gentleman of the interior.

sentences.zh: 慬有勇氣，常慷慨好功名。

translations.literal: Jin had courage and spirit and was often impassioned, loving merit and fame.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Jin was bold and hungry for renown.

sentences.zh: 初為車騎將軍鄧鴻司馬，再遷，延平元年拜西域副校尉。

translations.literal: At first he was major to General-in-chief Deng Hong; after two promotions, in the first year of Yanping he was appointed deputy colonel for the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: He began as Deng Hong's major, rose twice, and in Yanping 1 (106 CE) became deputy colonel for the west.

sentences.zh: 慬行至河西，會西域諸國反叛，攻都護任尚於疏勒。

translations.literal: When Jin reached Hexi, it happened that the various states of the Western Regions rebelled and attacked Protector-General Ren Shang at Shule.
translations.idiomatic: He reached Hexi as the western states rose and besieged Ren Shang in Shule.

sentences.zh: 尚上書求救，詔慬將河西四郡羌胡五千騎馳赴之，慬未至而尚已得解。

translations.literal: Shang submitted a memorial begging rescue; an edict ordered Jin to lead five thousand Qiang and Hu horse of the four Hexi commanderies to gallop to him; before Jin arrived Shang had already obtained relief.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Shang begged for aid; the court ordered Liang Jin to race west with five thousand Qiang and Hu cavalry from four Hexi commanderies, but Ren Shang broke the siege before he arrived.

sentences.zh: 會征尚還，以騎都尉段禧為都護，西域長史趙博為騎都尉。

translations.literal: It happened that Shang was recalled on campaign; Colonel of the Horse Duan Xi was made protector-general, and chief clerk of the Western Regions Zhao Bo was made colonel of the horse.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Shang was recalled; Duan Xi became protector-general and Zhao Bo western chief clerk, both with colonel of the horse rank.

sentences.zh: 禧、博守它干城。

translations.literal: Xi and Bo defended Tagan city.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Xi and Zhao Bo held Tagan.

sentences.zh: 它干城小，慬以為不可固，乃譎說龜茲王白霸，欲入共保其城，白霸許之。

translations.literal: Tagan city was small; Jin thought it could not be held firm and therefore deceived King Ba Ba of Qiuci, wishing to enter together and protect that city; Ba Ba consented.
translations.idiomatic: Tagan was too small to hold; Liang Jin talked King Ba Ba of Kucha into letting him move into the capital for mutual defense, and Ba Ba agreed.

sentences.zh: 吏人固諫，白霸不聽。

translations.literal: Clerks and people firmly remonstrated, but Ba Ba would not listen.
translations.idiomatic: Kucha's officials pleaded against it; Ba Ba refused.

sentences.zh: 慬既入，遣將急迎禧、博，合軍八九千人。

translations.literal: After Jin had entered, he sent generals urgently to welcome Xi and Bo, combining forces of eight or nine thousand men.
translations.idiomatic: Once inside, Liang Jin rushed escorts for Duan Xi and Zhao Bo and united some nine thousand men.

sentences.zh: 龜茲吏人並叛其王，而與溫宿、姑墨數萬兵反，共圍城。

translations.literal: Clerks and people of Qiuci together rebelled against their king and with tens of thousands of troops of Wensu and Gumo turned back and jointly besieged the city.
translations.idiomatic: The Kucheans mutinied against their king and, with tens of thousands from Wensu and Gumo, besieged the city.

sentences.zh: 慬等出戰，大破之。

translations.literal: Jin and the others went out to battle and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Jin sallied and broke them.

sentences.zh: 連兵數月，胡觿敗走，乘勝追擊，凡斬首萬餘級，獲生口數千人，駱駝畜產數萬頭，龜茲乃定。

translations.literal: Arms linked for several months, the Hu host was defeated and fled; riding victory they pursued and struck; in all they beheaded more than ten thousand ranks, captured several thousand living persons, and several tens of thousands of head of camels and livestock, and Qiuci was then settled.
translations.idiomatic: Months of fighting routed the allies; the pursuit took ten thousand heads, thousands of captives, and tens of thousands of camels and beasts, and Kucha was pacified.

sentences.zh: 而道路尚隔，檄書不通。

translations.literal: Yet the roads were still blocked and writs did not get through.
translations.idiomatic: The road east stayed closed; no courier reached the capital.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，朝廷憂之。

translations.literal: After more than a year the court worried over them.
translations.idiomatic: After a year the court grew anxious.

sentences.zh: 公卿議者以為西域阻遠，數有背叛，吏士屯田，其費無已。

translations.literal: Debating ministers held that the Western Regions were blocked and distant, repeatedly rebelled, and that clerks and soldiers on garrison farms cost without end.
translations.idiomatic: Ministers argued the west was distant, unruly, and the garrison farms bled the treasury dry.

sentences.zh: 永初元年，遂罷都護，遣騎都尉王弘發關中兵迎慬、禧、博及伊吾盧、柳中屯田吏士。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongchu, they thereupon abolished the protector-general; Colonel of the Horse Wang Hong was sent to mobilize Guanzhong troops to welcome Jin, Xi, Bo, and the garrison-farm clerks and soldiers of Yiwulu and Liuzhong.
translations.idiomatic: Yongchu 1 (107 CE): the protector-generalship was scrapped; Wang Hong led Guanzhong troops to evacuate Liang Jin, Duan Xi, Zhao Bo, and the colonists at Yiwulu and Liuzhong.

sentences.zh: 二年春，還至敦煌。

translations.literal: In the spring of the second year they returned to Dunhuang.
translations.idiomatic: The next spring they reached Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 會觿羌反叛，朝廷大發兵西擊之，逆詔慬留為諸軍援。

translations.literal: It happened that the Qiang host rebelled; the court greatly dispatched troops west to strike them and counter-ordered Jin to remain as support for the various armies.
translations.idiomatic: Then the Qiang rose; the court sent a great host west and ordered Liang Jin to stay as reserve for the armies.

sentences.zh: 慬至張掖日勒。

translations.literal: Jin reached Rile in Zhangye.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Jin reached Rile in Zhangye.

sentences.zh: [73]羌諸種萬餘人攻亭候，殺略吏人。

translations.literal: [73] More than ten thousand of the various Qiang stocks attacked post stations and killed and plundered clerks and people.
translations.idiomatic: [73] Over ten thousand Qiang clansmen stormed the watch-posts, killing and looting.

sentences.zh: 慬進兵擊，大破之，乘勝追至昭武，[74]虜遂散走，其能脫者十二三。

translations.literal: Jin advanced troops and struck, greatly defeated them, and riding victory pursued to Zhaowu; [74] the captives thereupon scattered and fled; those able to escape were only twelve or thirteen in ten.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Jin attacked, routed them, and chased them to Zhaowu; [74] only two or three in ten escaped.

sentences.zh: 及至姑臧，羌大豪三百餘人詣慬降，並尉譬遣還故地，河西四郡復安。

translations.literal: When they reached Guzang, more than three hundred great Qiang chieftains came to Jin to surrender; together he consoled them with orders and sent them back to their old lands, and the four Hexi commanderies were again secure.
translations.idiomatic: At Guzang three hundred Qiang headmen surrendered; Liang Jin sent them home with reassurances and Hexi was calm again.

sentences.zh: 慬受詔當屯金城，聞羌轉寇三輔，迫近園陵，即引兵赴擊之，轉戰武功美陽關。

translations.literal: Jin received orders to garrison at Jincheng; hearing the Qiang had turned to raid the three adjuncts, pressing near the imperial tombs, he immediately led troops to hurry and strike them, shifting battle to Meiyang pass at Wugong.
translations.idiomatic: Ordered to Jincheng, he heard the Qiang were threatening the capital tombs and raced to fight them on the Wugong–Meiyang road.

sentences.zh: [75]慬臨陣被創，不顧，連破走之，盡還得所掠生口，獲馬畜財物甚觿，羌遂奔散。

translations.literal: [75] Jin, facing the battle line, was wounded but paid no heed; repeatedly he broke and drove them off, fully recovering the living captives they had plundered, and captured horses, livestock, and goods in very great quantity; the Qiang thereupon fled scattered.
translations.idiomatic: [75] Wounded on the line, he fought on, broke the Qiang, freed every captive, and seized rich spoils of horse and herd until the tribes scattered.

sentences.zh: 朝廷嘉之，數璽書勞勉，委以西方事，令為諸軍節度。

translations.literal: The court praised him, repeatedly sending edicts of encouragement and entrusting him with western affairs, ordering him to command the various armies.
translations.idiomatic: The court praised him in repeated edicts, gave him overall command in the west, and set him over the armies.

sentences.zh: 三年冬，南單于與烏桓大人俱反。

translations.literal: In the winter of the third year the southern chanyu together with Wuhuan great men both rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Yongchu 3 winter: the southern chanyu and Wuhuan chiefs rose together.

sentences.zh: 以大司農何熙行車騎將軍事，中郎將龐雄為副，將羽林五校營士，及發緣邊十郡兵二萬餘人，[76]又遼東太守耿夔率將鮮卑種觿共擊之，詔慬行度遼將軍事。

translations.literal: The grand minister of agriculture He Xi was put in charge of the affairs of general-in-chief and chariots and cavalry, with gentleman of the interior Pang Xiong as deputy, leading picked men of the five-camp guard and mobilizing more than twenty thousand troops of ten border commanderies, [76] and moreover Liaodong governor Geng Kui led and brought Xianbei tribal hosts jointly to strike them; an edict ordered Jin to act as general on the Liao campaign.
translations.idiomatic: He Xi directed the campaign with Pang Xiong as deputy, twenty thousand men from ten border commanderies, [76] and Geng Kui of Liaodong led Xianbei auxiliaries; Liang Jin was named acting general on the Liao.

sentences.zh: 龐雄與耿夔共擊匈奴奧鞬日逐王，破之。

translations.literal: Pang Xiong together with Geng Kui jointly struck the Xiongnu Prince Aojian of the left and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Xiong and Geng Kui crushed the Xiongnu Aojian prince of the left.

sentences.zh: 單于乃自將圍中郎將耿種於美稷，連戰數月，攻之轉急，種移檄求救。

translations.literal: The chanyu thereupon personally besieged Colonel of the Interior Geng Zhong at Meiji, fighting connectedly for several months, the attack growing more urgent; Zhong sent dispatches begging rescue.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu then besieged Geng Zhong at Meiji for months; Geng Zhong called for help.

sentences.zh: 明年正月，慬將八千餘人馳往赴之，至屬國故城，與匈奴左將軍、烏桓大人戰，破斬其渠帥，殺三千餘人，虜其妻子，獲財物甚觿。

translations.literal: In the first month of the next year Jin led more than eight thousand men galloping to go to them; reaching the old city of the dependent state, he fought the Xiongnu general of the left and the Wuhuan great man, broke them, beheaded their chieftain, killed more than three thousand, took their wives and children, and captured goods in very great quantity.
translations.idiomatic: The next first month Liang Jin flew to the old dependent-state fort with eight thousand men, routed the Xiongnu left general and Wuhuan chiefs, took three thousand heads, captives, and rich booty.

sentences.zh: 單于復自將七八千騎迎攻，圍慬。

translations.literal: The chanyu again personally led seven or eight thousand horse to meet and attack, besieging Jin.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu counterattacked with seven or eight thousand riders and surrounded Liang Jin.

sentences.zh: 慬被甲奔擊，所向皆破，虜遂引還虎澤。

translations.literal: Jin, armored, charged and struck; wherever he turned all were broken; the barbarians thereupon drew back to Huzhe marsh.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Jin in armor charged every point of the ring until the enemy broke and fled to Huzhe marsh.

sentences.zh: 三月，何熙軍到五原曼柏，[77]暴疾，不能進，遣龐雄與慬及耿種步騎萬六千人攻虎澤。

translations.literal: In the third month He Xi's army reached Manbai in Wuyuan; [77] he suddenly fell ill and could not advance; he sent Pang Xiong with Jin and Geng Zhong, sixteen thousand infantry and cavalry, to attack Huzhe.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month He Xi reached Manbai in Wuyuan [77] but fell mortally ill; he sent Pang Xiong, Liang Jin, and Geng Zhong with sixteen thousand men against Huzhe.

sentences.zh: 連營稍前，單于惶怖，遣左奧鞬日逐王詣慬乞降，慬乃大陳兵受之。

translations.literal: Linked camps advanced slightly; the chanyu was frightened and terrified, sent the Prince Aojian of the left to Jin to beg surrender, and Jin thereupon greatly arrayed troops and received them.
translations.idiomatic: The linked camps crept forward; the chanyu panicked and sent the left Aojian prince to sue for surrender; Liang Jin received the capitulation in full battle order.

sentences.zh: 單于脫帽徒跣，面縛稽顙，納質。

translations.literal: The chanyu removed his cap, went barefoot, came bound with hands behind, knocked his forehead to the ground, and sent hostages.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu doffed cap and shoes, bound his hands, kowtowed, and sent hostages.

sentences.zh: 會熙卒於師，即拜慬度遼將軍。

translations.literal: It happened that Xi died in the army; Jin was at once appointed general on the Liao.
translations.idiomatic: He Xi died in camp; Liang Jin was named general on the Liao at once.

sentences.zh: 龐雄還為大鴻臚。

translations.literal: Pang Xiong returned as grand herald.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Xiong became grand herald.

sentences.zh: 雄，巴郡人，有勇略，稱為名將。

translations.literal: Xiong was a man of Ba commandery, had courage and stratagem, and was called a famous general.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Xiong of Ba commandery was reckoned a famous general for courage and craft.

sentences.zh: 明年，安定、北地、上郡皆被羌寇，谷貴人流，不能自立。

translations.literal: The next year Anding, Beidi, and Shang commandery all suffered Qiang raids; grain was costly and people fled, unable to establish themselves.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Anding, Beidi, and Shang were overrun; famine drove the people from their homes.

sentences.zh: 詔慬發邊兵迎三郡太守，使將吏人徙扶風界。

translations.literal: An edict ordered Jin to dispatch border troops to welcome the three commanderies' governors and ordered them to lead clerks and people to migrate within the bounds of Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: He was told to send border troops to escort the three governors and move their people into Fufeng.

sentences.zh: 慬即遣南單于兄子優孤塗奴將兵迎之。

translations.literal: Jin immediately sent the southern chanyu's nephew Yougu Tunu with troops to welcome them.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Jin sent the chanyu's nephew Yougu Tunu with troops to escort them.

sentences.zh: 既還，慬以塗奴接其家屬有勞，輒授以羌侯印綬，坐專擅，征下獄，抵罪。

translations.literal: After they returned, Jin because Tunu had met their families with merit abruptly invested him with the seal and ribbon of Qiang marquis—sitting for acting on his own authority he was summoned and cast into prison and punished for his crime.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Liang Jin gave Tunu a Qiang marquis seal for escorting the families—an unauthorized enfeoffment—and was jailed for it.

sentences.zh: 明年，校書郎馬融上書訟慬與護羌校尉龐參，有詔原刑。

translations.literal: The next year collator Ma Rong submitted a memorial pleading for Jin and Colonel Who Protects the Qiang Pang Can; an edict pardoned their sentence.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Ma Rong memorialized for Liang Jin and Pang Can; the throne remitted their punishment.

sentences.zh: 語在龐參傳。

translations.literal: The words are in the biography of Pang Can.
translations.idiomatic: The story is told in the biography of Pang Can.

sentences.zh: 會叛羌寇三輔，關中盜賊起，拜慬謁者，將兵擊之。

translations.literal: It happened that rebel Qiang raided the three adjuncts and bandits rose in Guanzhong; Jin was appointed herald and sent with troops to strike them.
translations.idiomatic: When Qiang rebels threatened Guanzhong, Liang Jin was named herald and sent against them.

sentences.zh: 至湖縣，病卒。

translations.literal: When he reached Hu county he fell ill and died.
translations.idiomatic: He died of illness at Hu county.

sentences.zh: 何熙字孟孫，陳國人。

translations.literal: He Xi, courtesy name Mengsun, was a man of Chen.
translations.idiomatic: He Xi, style Mengsun, was a native of Chen.

sentences.zh: 少有大志。

translations.literal: In youth he had great ambitions.
translations.idiomatic: Even as a youth he aimed high.

sentences.zh: 永元中，為謁者。

translations.literal: In the Yongyuan period he served as herald.
translations.idiomatic: Under Yongyuan he became a herald.

sentences.zh: 身長八尺五寸，善為威容，贊拜殿中，音動左右。

translations.literal: His body was eight chi five cun in height; he was good at imposing bearing, assisting the obeisances in the hall, his voice moving those left and right.
translations.idiomatic: He stood eight chi five cun tall, carried himself with majesty in court rituals, and his voice filled the hall.

sentences.zh: 和帝偉之，擢為御史中丞，歷司隸校尉、大司農。

translations.literal: Emperor He thought him remarkable and promoted him to palace assistant censor; he passed through metropolitan commandant and grand minister of agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor He admired him and raised him to palace assistant censor, then metropolitan commandant, then grand minister of agriculture.

sentences.zh: 及在軍臨歿，遺言薄葬。

translations.literal: When in the army facing death he left instructions for a simple burial.
translations.idiomatic: Facing death in the field he asked only for a plain burial.

sentences.zh: 三子：臨，瑾，阜。

translations.literal: Three sons: Lin, Jin, and Fu.
translations.idiomatic: He had three sons: Lin, Jin, and Fu.

sentences.zh: 臨、瑾並有政能。

translations.literal: Lin and Jin both had administrative ability.
translations.idiomatic: Lin and Jin both proved able administrators.

sentences.zh: 阜俊才早沒。

translations.literal: Fu was a fine talent who died early.
translations.idiomatic: Fu, a brilliant son, died young.

sentences.zh: 臨子衡，為尚書，以正直稱，坐訟李膺等下獄，免官，廢於家。

translations.literal: Lin's son Heng became secretary; he was praised for integrity and straightforwardness; because he sued on behalf of Li Ying and others he was cast into prison, dismissed from office, and cast aside at home.
translations.idiomatic: Lin's son Heng rose to secretary, famed for integrity; he defended Li Ying, went to prison, was dismissed, and lived out his days in retirement.

sentences.zh: 論曰：時政平則文德用，而武略之士無所奮其力能，故漢世有發憤張膽，爭膏身於夷狄以要功名，多矣。

translations.literal: The judgment says: When government is calm then civil virtue is employed, and men of martial strategy have nowhere to vent their strength and ability; therefore in Han times there were many who in indignation opened their gall bladders and vied to smear their persons on the Yi and Di to seek merit and fame.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: In times of peace civil arts rule and soldiers have no outlet—so Han produced many who gladly risked the barbarian wastes for a name.

sentences.zh: 祭肜、耿秉啟匈奴之權，班超、梁慬奮西域之略，卒能成功立名，享受爵位，薦功祖廟，勒勳於後，亦一時之志士也。

translations.literal: Zhai Tong and Geng Bing opened the Xiongnu's power; Ban Chao and Liang Jin exerted strategy on the Western Regions; in the end they were able to complete merit and establish fame, enjoy noble rank, present merit at the ancestral temple, and inscribe their deeds for posterity—they too were men of aspiration for their age.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Tong and Geng Bing broke the Xiongnu; Ban Chao and Liang Jin carved a path through the west—all won titles, offered victory at the shrines, and left their names for later ages: true men of their day.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：定遠慷慨，專功西遐。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Dingyuan was impassioned and devoted his merit to the far west.
translations.idiomatic: The verse says: The Marquis of Dingyuan burned with zeal and staked his fame on the farthest west.

sentences.zh: 坦步□、雪，咫尺龍沙。

translations.literal: He strode the Pamirs and snowfields; the Dragon Sands lay within inches.
translations.idiomatic: He strode the Pamirs and the snow-line; the Dragon Sands lay within a stone's throw.

sentences.zh: [78]慬亦抗憤，勇乃負荷。

translations.literal: [78] Jin likewise bore high indignation; Yong then bore the burden.
translations.idiomatic: [78] Liang Jin matched that fire; Ban Yong shouldered the load after him.

translations.literal: [79] The transmitted text marks an editorial note here without added wording.
translations.idiomatic: [79] The edition carries only this annotation marker at this point in the verse.

sentences.zh: 校勘記

translations.literal: Collation notes
translations.idiomatic: Textual collation notes

sentences.zh: 一五七一頁三行：扶風平陵人。

translations.literal: Page 1571 line 3: a man of Pingling in Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Collation note: the line reads 'a man of Pingling in Fufeng.'

sentences.zh: 按：《班彪傳》云扶風安陵人，錢大昕謂當有一誤。

translations.literal: Comment: The "Biography of Ban Biao" says he was a man of Anling in Fufeng; Qian Daxin holds that one of the two must be wrong.
translations.idiomatic: The Ban Biao biography gives Anling in Fufeng; Qian Daxin argues one reading must be erroneous.

sentences.zh: 校補引柳從辰說，謂《東觀記》載班超亦為安陵人，則作「平陵」者誤。

translations.literal: The Jiaobu cites Liu Congchen, who says the Dongguan ji also records Ban Chao as from Anling, so the reading "Pingling" is mistaken.
translations.idiomatic: Supplementary collation cites Liu Congchen: the Dongguan ji likewise calls Ban Chao an Anling native, so "Pingling" is the error.

sentences.zh: 一五七一頁一〇行：超持《公羊春秋》。

translations.literal: Page 1571 line 10: Chao held the Gongyang commentary on the Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: Collation line: Ban Chao studied the Gongyang commentary on the Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 按：王先謙謂「持」當為「治」，避唐高宗諱改。

translations.literal: Comment: Wang Xianqian says "held" should read "studied," altered to avoid the taboo on Emperor Gaozong of Tang's name.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Xianqian: the graph "hold" stands for "study," changed under Tang taboo.

sentences.zh: 一五七四頁一行：西與姑墨接。

translations.literal: Page 1574 line 1: on the west it borders Gumo.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "borders Gumo on the west."

sentences.zh: 按：校補謂《前書》《西域傳》作「北與姑墨接」。

translations.literal: Comment: The Jiaobu says the Former Han "Treatise on the Western Regions" reads "on the north it borders Gumo."
translations.idiomatic: Collation note: the Han shu western treatise has "north," not "west."

sentences.zh: 一五七四頁二行：東西六千餘里。

translations.literal: Page 1574 line 2: east-west more than six thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "more than six thousand li from east to west."

sentences.zh: 按：「千」原作「十」，逕改正。

translations.literal: Comment: "Thousand" was originally written "ten"; it was corrected directly.
translations.idiomatic: Editorial note: the manuscript had "ten" for "thousand"; the text was emended.

sentences.zh: 一五七四頁二行：傍南山北波河西行。

translations.literal: Page 1574 line 2: along the south mountains north of the bo river traveling west.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "going west along the south range north of the bo channel."

sentences.zh: 按：《西域傳》「波」作「陂」。

translations.literal: Comment: In the "Treatise on the Western Regions" "bo" is written "pi" (slope).
translations.idiomatic: The western treatise writes "pi" (embankment) for "bo."

sentences.zh: 下一二行注「隨北山波河西行」同。

translations.literal: The next twelve lines, note "following the north mountains along the west bank," same case.
translations.idiomatic: Same gloss applies to the note twelve lines below.

sentences.zh: 一五七四頁六行：逆遣吏田慮先往降之。

translations.literal: Page 1574 line 6: he sent forward in advance clerk Tian Lü to go first and summon submission.
translations.idiomatic: Collation line on Tian Lü sent ahead to treat for surrender.

sentences.zh: 〈袁宏紀〉

translations.literal: Yuan Hong ji
translations.idiomatic: From the Annals of the Later Han by Yuan Hong

sentences.zh: 「田慮」作「陳憲」。

translations.literal: It writes "Chen Xian" for the name.
translations.idiomatic: That text reads "Chen Xian" instead.

sentences.zh: 惠棟謂古陳田字通，「憲」當為「慮」，字之誤也。

translations.literal: Hui Dong says the ancient graphs Chen and Tian were interchangeable; "Xian" should be "Lü," a graphic error.
translations.idiomatic: Hui Dong: Chen and Tian could interchange; "Xian" is a corruption of "Lü."

sentences.zh: 今按：慮憲形近，未知孰是。

translations.literal: Present comment: Lü and Xian are graphically close; it is not known which is correct.
translations.idiomatic: The forms are easily confused; the true reading remains uncertain.

sentences.zh: 一五七四頁一二行：今龜音丘勿反。

translations.literal: Page 1575 line 1: now for Qi the pronunciation is qiu-wu fan.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: note on reading Qi (Kucha).

sentences.zh: 按：龜無入聲，「勿」字疑鬥。

translations.literal: Comment: Qi has no entering tone; the character "wu" in the fanqie is suspected to be a corruption of "dou."
translations.idiomatic: Phonological note: the fanqie may be corrupt—"wu" for "dou."

sentences.zh: 一五七五頁一行：超守盤橐城。

translations.literal: Page 1575 line 1: Chao defended Pancheng city.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "Chao held Pancheng."

sentences.zh: 按：汲本、殿本「盤」作「盤」。

translations.literal: Comment: In the Ji edition and the Palace edition "pan" is written "pan" (same character variant).
translations.idiomatic: Ji and Palace editions use the same character form for "Pan."

sentences.zh: 一五八〇頁二行：本或作植。

translations.literal: Page 1580 line 2: some editions write zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: alternate reading "zhi."

sentences.zh: 按：通鑒胡注引「植」作「楨」，胡氏謂案《西域傳》，靈帝建寧三年，涼州刺史孟佗遣兵討疏勒，攻楨中城，「楨中」是也。

translations.literal: Comment: Hu Sanxing's Tongjian commentary cites "zhi" as "zhen"; Hu says checking the western treatise, in the third year of Jianning the Liangzhou governor Meng Tuo sent troops against Shule and attacked Zhenzhong city—"Zhenzhong" is correct.
translations.idiomatic: Hu Sanxing argues for "Zhenzhong" from the western treatise parallel.

sentences.zh: 一五八〇頁一三行：必從龜茲求救。

translations.literal: Page 1580 line 13: he was certain to seek rescue from Qiuci.
translations.idiomatic: Collation line on seeking Kucha's aid.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引惠棟說，謂袁宏紀「救」作「食」。

translations.literal: Comment: The Jiji cites Hui Dong: Yuan Hong's record writes "food" instead of "rescue."
translations.idiomatic: Variant: Yuan Hong has "food" for "rescue."

sentences.zh: 一五八一頁七行：遣其左將北鞬支。

translations.literal: Page 1581 line 7: he sent his general of the left Beijianzhi.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the left general Beijianzhi.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引惠棟說，謂「北」一作「比」。

translations.literal: Comment: The Jiji cites Hui Dong: "north" is sometimes written "bi."
translations.idiomatic: Some manuscripts write "Bi" for "Bei."

sentences.zh: 校補引錢大昭說，謂閩本作「比」。

translations.literal: The Jiaobu cites Qian Dazhao: the Min edition writes "bi."
translations.idiomatic: Qian Dazhao notes the Min edition has "bi."

sentences.zh: 一五八一頁一四行：

translations.literal: Page 1581 line 14:
translations.idiomatic: Page 1581 line 14: (lemma only in the edition).

translations.literal: (zheng)
translations.idiomatic: (Collation marker in the received text.)

sentences.zh: 營大澤中。

translations.literal: Encamped in a great marsh.
translations.idiomatic: Continuation: "encamped in a broad marsh."

sentences.zh: 按：刊誤謂案文「正」當作「止」。

translations.literal: Comment: Kanwu says checking the text "zheng" should be "zhi" (stop).
translations.idiomatic: Kanwu emends "zheng" to "zhi" (halted).

sentences.zh: 集解引惠棟說，謂案

translations.literal: The Jiji cites Hui Dong, saying checking
translations.idiomatic: Hui Dong in the Jiji argues that examining

sentences.zh: 〈袁宏紀〉

translations.literal: Yuan Hong ji
translations.idiomatic: the Yuan Hong ji

sentences.zh: ，「正」字當衍。

translations.literal: the character "zheng" should be excised as redundant.
translations.idiomatic: the character "zheng" is a stray addition.

sentences.zh: 今依惠說刪「正」字。

translations.literal: Now following Hui's theory the character "zheng" is deleted.
translations.idiomatic: The edition deletes "zheng" per Hui Dong.

sentences.zh: 一五八二頁一行：尉犁王泛及北鞬支等三十人。

translations.literal: Page 1582 line 1: King Fan of Yuli and Beijianzhi and thirty others.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: King Fan of Yuli, Beijianzhi, and thirty men.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引王補說，謂袁宏紀「泛」作「沉」。

translations.literal: Comment: The Jiji cites Wang Bu: Yuan Hong's record writes "Chen" for Fan.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Bu: Yuan Hong writes "Chen" for the king's name.

sentences.zh: 又引惠棟說，謂

translations.literal: It also cites Hui Dong, saying
translations.idiomatic: Hui Dong adds

sentences.zh: 〈袁紀〉

translations.literal: Yuan ji
translations.idiomatic: the Yuan ji

sentences.zh: 作「四十一人」。

translations.literal: writes "forty-one persons."
translations.idiomatic: gives "forty-one persons" instead of thirty.

sentences.zh: 一五八二頁八行：先帝深愍邊萌嬰羅寇害。

translations.literal: Page 1582 line 8: the former emperor deeply pitied the border sprouts who suffered the harm of invaders.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "pitied the border folk caught in invasion."

sentences.zh: 「萌」汲本、殿本作「氓」。

translations.literal: "Meng" in the Ji and Palace editions is written "mang."
translations.idiomatic: Ji and Palace read "mang" for "meng."

sentences.zh: 今按：「氓」亦作「萌」，音義並同。

translations.literal: Present comment: "Mang" is also written "meng"; sound and sense are the same.
translations.idiomatic: "Mang" and "meng" are equivalent here.

sentences.zh: 又「羅」汲本、殿本作「罹」。

translations.literal: Also "luo" in the Ji and Palace editions is written "li" (suffer).
translations.idiomatic: Ji and Palace use "li" (suffer) for "luo."

sentences.zh: 今按：羅罹通。

translations.literal: Present comment: luo and li are interchangeable.
translations.idiomatic: "Luo" and "li" are variant graphs for the same word.

sentences.zh: 一五八三頁一二行：正丘首[正首]丘也。

translations.literal: Page 1583 line 12: "turn the head toward the mound" [correct text: "turn the head"] toward the mound.
translations.idiomatic: Collation on the fox proverb; bracketed correction follows the Jiji.

sentences.zh: 據集解本補，與禮檀弓鄭注合。

translations.literal: Supplied from the Jiji edition, matching Zheng Xuan's gloss on Tan Gong in the Li ji.
translations.idiomatic: The supplement aligns with Zheng Xuan's Li ji commentary.

sentences.zh: 一五八五頁九行：《周禮》

translations.literal: Page 1585 line 9: the Zhou li
translations.idiomatic: Collation: opening citation of the Zhou li

translations.literal: (qing)
translations.idiomatic: (Collation: "qing" for "xiang" in the manuscript.)

sentences.zh: [鄉]大夫職。

translations.literal: [xiang] grandee's duties.
translations.idiomatic: Bracketed emendation: "grandee of the district," per the Zhou li office.

sentences.zh: 據殿本改。

translations.literal: Changed according to the Palace edition.
translations.idiomatic: Emended from the Palace edition.

sentences.zh: 一五八五頁一〇行：即知

translations.literal: Page 1585 line 10: then knowing
translations.idiomatic: Collation fragment: "then knowing"

translations.literal: (one)
translations.idiomatic: (Collation marker: "one" for "twenty" in a numeral.)

sentences.zh: [二十]與《周禮》七尺。

translations.literal: [twenty] matches the Zhou li seven chi.
translations.idiomatic: Emended numeral to match the Zhou li seven-chi measure.

sentences.zh: 同據刊誤改。

translations.literal: Likewise changed according to Kanwu.
translations.idiomatic: Kanwu emendation applied here as well.

sentences.zh: 一五八五頁一〇行：[周]禮國中六十免役。

translations.literal: Page 1585 line 10: [Zhou] li: at sixty in the capital district one is exempted from labor.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Zhou li passage on exemption at sixty.

sentences.zh: 據刊誤補。

translations.literal: Supplied according to Kanwu.
translations.idiomatic: Supplied per Kanwu.

sentences.zh: 一五八六頁六行：以戊己校尉任尚為都護。

translations.literal: Page 1586 line 6: Ren Shang of the Wu and Ji colonelcy was made protector-general.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Ren Shang as Wu-Ji colonel becomes protector-general.

sentences.zh: 按：刊誤謂是時但有戊校尉，多「己」字。

translations.literal: Comment: Kanwu says at this time there was only the Wu colonel; the character "ji" is redundant.
translations.idiomatic: Kanwu: only the Wu colonel existed—the "ji" is superfluous.

sentences.zh: 一五八六頁六行：尚謂超曰。

translations.literal: Page 1586 line 6: Shang said to Chao.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "Shang said to Chao."

sentences.zh: 按：集解引王補說，謂

translations.literal: Comment: The Jiji cites Wang Bu, saying
translations.idiomatic: Wang Bu in the Jiji notes

sentences.zh: 〈袁宏紀〉

translations.literal: Yuan Hong ji
translations.idiomatic: the Yuan Hong ji

sentences.zh: 作「尚與超書曰」。

translations.literal: writes "Shang wrote a letter to Chao saying."
translations.idiomatic: has this as a letter from Shang to Chao.

sentences.zh: 一五八六頁七行：超曰。

translations.literal: Page 1586 line 7: Chao said.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "Chao said."

sentences.zh: 按：集解引王補說，謂據

translations.literal: Comment: The Jiji cites Wang Bu, saying according to
translations.idiomatic: Wang Bu argues from

sentences.zh: 〈袁紀〉

translations.literal: Yuan ji
translations.idiomatic: the Yuan ji

sentences.zh: 尚與超書，則超此語亦荅書，非面論也。

translations.literal: that Shang wrote to Chao, so this reply of Chao's is also a letter answer, not a face-to-face discussion.
translations.idiomatic: that Ban Chao's words are a written reply, not a conversation.

sentences.zh: 一五八七頁三行：元初六年至遂共攻沒班。

translations.literal: Page 1587 line 3: from the sixth year of Yuanchu to "thereupon jointly attacked and destroyed Ban."
translations.idiomatic: Collation block on the Suo Ban incident dating.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引通鑒考異，謂案

translations.literal: Comment: The Jiji cites the Tongjian Kaoyi, saying checking
translations.idiomatic: The Kaoyi checks

sentences.zh: 〈本紀〉

translations.literal: the basic annals
translations.idiomatic: the annals

sentences.zh: 及

translations.literal: and
translations.idiomatic: and

sentences.zh: 〈車師傳〉

translations.literal: the "Treatise on Cheshi"
translations.idiomatic: the Cheshi treatise

sentences.zh: ，皆云永寧元年事，蓋班以去年末屯伊吾，今春見殺，或今春奏事方到也。

translations.literal: all call it an event of the first year of Yongning; presumably Ban garrisoned Yiwu at the year's end and was killed this spring, or the memorial reached court only this spring.
translations.idiomatic: which date the affair to Yongning 1: Ban wintered at Yiwu and was killed the next spring, or news arrived that spring.

sentences.zh: 一五八八頁六行：後置副校[尉]於車師。

translations.literal: Page 1588 line 6: afterward placed the deputy colonel [wei] at Cheshi.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: bracketed "wei" (colonel) supplied.

sentences.zh: 據汲本、殿本補。

translations.literal: Supplied from the Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Added from Ji and Palace.

sentences.zh: 一五八八頁九行：廷尉綦母參。

translations.literal: Page 1588 line 9: Commandant of the Court Qimu Can.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Qimu Can as commandant of the court.

sentences.zh: 按：集解本「母」作「毋」，校補謂據《通鑒》正。

translations.literal: Comment: The Jiji edition writes "wu" for "mu"; the Jiaobu says correct per the Tongjian.
translations.idiomatic: Jiji writes "Qiwu"; Jiaobu follows the Zizhi tongjian spelling.

sentences.zh: 一五八八頁一二行：虜埶

translations.literal: Page 1588 line 12: the barbarian situation
translations.idiomatic: Collation: "the barbarian situation"

translations.literal: (bi)
translations.idiomatic: (Collation: spurious "must" inserted in some texts.)

sentences.zh: 弱則為患微矣。

translations.literal: weak then the harm they cause is slight.
translations.idiomatic: If they are weak, the threat they pose is slight.

sentences.zh: 據刊誤刪。

translations.literal: Deleted according to Kanwu.
translations.idiomatic: Kanwu deletes the spurious character.

sentences.zh: 涉如涉水，獵如獵獸。

translations.literal: "She" is like wading water; "lie" is like hunting beasts.
translations.idiomatic: Gloss: "peruse" likened to wading and hunting—skimming, not deep study.

sentences.zh: 言不能周悉，粗窺覽之也。

translations.literal: It means he could not exhaustively know them but roughly surveyed them.
translations.idiomatic: He skimmed widely rather than mastering every detail.

sentences.zh: 《東觀記》曰：「超持公羊春秋，多所窺覽。」

translations.literal: The Dongguan ji says: "Chao held the Gongyang Spring and Autumn and perused widely."
translations.idiomatic: Dongguan ji parallel: Ban Chao read the Gongyang and browsed widely.

sentences.zh: 校書郎，解見

translations.literal: Collator in the palace library—explanation seen in
translations.idiomatic: On collator, see

sentences.zh: 〈班固傳〉

translations.literal: the "Biography of Ban Gu"
translations.idiomatic: the biography of Ban Gu
End of cross-reference.

sentences.zh: 傅介子，北地人。

translations.literal: Fu Jiezi, a man of Beidi.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Jiezi of Beidi.

sentences.zh: 昭帝時使西域，刺殺樓蘭王，封義陽侯。

translations.literal: In Emperor Zhao's time he was sent to the Western Regions and assassinated the king of Loulan; he was enfeoffed as marquis of Yiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Zhao he killed the Loulan king in the west and was made marquis of Yiyang.

sentences.zh: 張騫，漢中人，武帝時鑿空開西域，封博望侯。

translations.literal: Zhang Qian, a man of Hanzhong; in Emperor Wu's time he opened a void path to the Western Regions and was enfeoffed as marquis of Bowang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qian of Hanzhong opened the west for Emperor Wu and became marquis of Bowang.

sentences.zh: 《續漢書》作「久弄筆研乎」。

translations.literal: The Xihan shu reads "long fiddling with brush and inkstone."
translations.idiomatic: Xu Han shu variant: "long fiddling with brush and inkstone."

sentences.zh: 《華嶠書》作「久事筆耕乎」。

translations.literal: The Hua Qiao shu reads "long serving at brush-plowing."
translations.idiomatic: Hua Qiao shu variant: "long at brush-plowing."

sentences.zh: 研音硯。

translations.literal: "Yan" is pronounced like "yan" (inkstone).
translations.idiomatic: Note: "yan" (grind) is the inkstone word.

sentences.zh: 一坐所尊，則先祭酒。

translations.literal: In a seated group the one honored pours the libation first—hence "libationer."
translations.idiomatic: The libationer was the senior who poured the first cup.

sentences.zh: 今稱祭酒，相尊敬之詞也。

translations.literal: Now calling someone "libationer" is a term of respect.
translations.idiomatic: Later usage made "libationer" a polite address.

sentences.zh: 《續漢志》曰：「蘭台令史六人，秩百石，掌書劾奏及印主文書。」

translations.literal: The Xu Han zhi says: "Six Lan Terrace clerks, rank one hundred piculs, in charge of memorials for impeachment and seals and chief documents."
translations.idiomatic: Xu Han zhi on the six Lan Terrace clerks at one hundred piculs.

sentences.zh: 伊吾，匈奴中地名，在今伊州納職縣界。

translations.literal: Yiwu is a place name among the Xiongnu, now within the border of Nazhi county in Yi prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: Yiwu: Xiongnu toponym, near modern Hami region.

sentences.zh: 《前書》音義曰「蒲類，匈奴中海名，在敦煌北」也。

translations.literal: The Former Han "Sounds and meanings" says "Pulei is a sea name among the Xiongnu, north of Dunhuang."
translations.idiomatic: Han shu gloss: Pulei (Barkol) is the northern Xiongnu sea by Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 鄯善本西域樓蘭國也，昭帝元鳳四年改為鄯善。

translations.literal: Shanshan was originally the Loulan state of the Western Regions; in the fourth year of Yuanfeng of Emperor Zhao it was changed to Shanshan.
translations.idiomatic: Shanshan was Loulan, renamed in Yuanfeng 4 of Emperor Zhao.

sentences.zh: 去陽關一千六百里，去長安六千一百里也。

translations.literal: It is one thousand six hundred li from Yang Pass and six thousand one hundred li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Distance: 1,600 li from Yang Pass, 6,100 li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 曹，輩也。

translations.literal: Cao means 'company' or 'class.'
translations.idiomatic: Here the word means 'you gentlemen'—his comrades.

sentences.zh: 《東觀記》曰「斬得匈奴節使屋賴帶、副使比離支首及節」也。

translations.literal: The Dongguan ji says: 'They beheaded the Xiongnu envoy bearing the staff, Wulaidai, and the deputy envoy Bilizhi, and took the staff.'
translations.idiomatic: Dongguan ji records the heads of envoys Wulaidai and Bilizhi and the captured staff.

sentences.zh: 于窴國去長安九千六百七十里，南與婼羌，西與姑墨接。

translations.literal: The state of Yutian is nine thousand six hundred seventy li from Chang'an; on the south it borders Ruoqiang, on the west it borders Gumo.
translations.idiomatic: Khotan lies 9,670 li from Chang'an, bordering Ruoqiang south and Gumo west.

sentences.zh: 莎車國去長安九千九百五十里。

translations.literal: The state of Kasha is nine thousand nine hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Kasha is 9,950 li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 西城南北有大山，中央有河，東西六千餘里。

translations.literal: The text reads 'western city' (xi cheng), evidently meaning the Western Regions: great mountains lie north and south; in the center is a river; from east to west it is more than six thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: The Western Regions are boxed by ranges north and south, with a central river and more than six thousand li from east to west.

sentences.zh: 東至玉門、陽關有兩道，從鄯善傍南山北波河西行，至莎車，為南道。

translations.literal: East to Jade Gate and Yang Pass are two routes: from Shanshan along the south mountains north of the bo channel westward to Kasha is the southern route.
translations.idiomatic: The southern road runs from Shanshan west along the southern range north of the river channel to Kasha.

sentences.zh: 雄張猶熾盛也。

translations.literal: Xiongzhang means blazing and flourishing.
translations.idiomatic: The phrase means to lord it over others or throw one's weight about.

sentences.zh: 張音丁亮反。

translations.literal: Zhang is read with the fanqie ding-liang.
translations.idiomatic: Comment on the pronunciation of the graph (first syllable zhang).

sentences.zh: 波，傍也。

translations.literal: Bo means 'alongside.'
translations.idiomatic: The graph is read in the sense of 'alongside' the river.

sentences.zh: 波音詖。

translations.literal: Bo is read like bi (erroneous graph for 'side').
translations.idiomatic: Phonetic gloss: read like the homophone meaning 'side' or 'slant.'

sentences.zh: 《續漢》及《華嶠書》「騧」字並作「騩」。

translations.literal: The Xihan shu and Hua Qiao shu both write the 'dapple-gray' graph as gui.
translations.idiomatic: Commentaries alternate graphs for the dapple mount.

sentences.zh: 說文：「馬淺黑色也。

translations.literal: The Shuowen says: 'A horse of pale black color.'
translations.idiomatic: Shuowen: a gray-black horse.

sentences.zh: 」音京媚反。

translations.literal: It is read jing-mei fan.
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie reading for the horse name.

sentences.zh: 龜茲國居居延城，去長安七千四百八十里，南與精絕，東與且末，北與烏孫，西與姑墨接。

translations.literal: The state of Qiuci dwells at Juyan city, seven thousand four hundred eighty li from Chang'an; on the south it borders Jingjue, on the east Qiemi, on the north Wusun, on the west Gumo.
translations.idiomatic: Kucha: capital Juyan, 7,480 li from Chang'an; borders given for four directions.

sentences.zh: 《前書》音義龜茲音丘慈。

translations.literal: The Former Han 'Sounds and meanings' read Qiuci as qiu-ci.
translations.idiomatic: Han shu gloss: Kucha as qiu-ci.

sentences.zh: 今龜音丘勿反，茲音沮惟反，蓋急言耳。

translations.literal: Now qi is read qiu-wu fan, ci is read ju-wei fan—this is hurried speech.
translations.idiomatic: Later fanqie gives the modern pronunciation cluster.

sentences.zh: 自車師前王庭隨北山波河西行，至疏勒，為北道。

translations.literal: From the court of the forward king of Cheshi follow the north mountains along the west bank to Shule—that is the northern route.
translations.idiomatic: The northern road runs from Forward Cheshi along the northern range to Shule.

sentences.zh: 疏勒國居疏勒城，去長安九千三百五十里也。

translations.literal: The state of Shule dwells at Shule city, nine thousand three hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Shule's capital is 9,350 li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 《續漢書》曰「求得故王兄子榆勒立之，更名曰忠」也。

translations.literal: The Xihan shu says: 'They found the former king's elder brother's son Yu Le and established him, changing his name to Zhong.'
translations.idiomatic: Xu Han shu: the restored heir Yu Le was renamed Zhong.

sentences.zh: 焉耆國居員渠城，去長安七千三百里，北與烏孫接。

translations.literal: The state of Yanqi dwells at Yuanqu city, seven thousand three hundred li from Chang'an; on the north it borders Wusun.
translations.idiomatic: Yanqi: Yuanqu, 7,300 li from Chang'an, borders Wusun north.

sentences.zh: 姑墨國王居南城，去長安八千一百五十里。

translations.literal: The king of Gumo dwells at the southern city, eight thousand one hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Gumo king at southern city, 8,150 li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 尉頭國居尉頭谷，去長八千六百五十里，南與疏勒接。

translations.literal: The state of Weitou dwells at Weitou valley, eight thousand six hundred fifty li from Chang'an; on the south it borders Shule.
translations.idiomatic: Weitou in its gorge, 8,650 li from Chang'an, borders Shule south.

sentences.zh: 衣服類烏孫也。

translations.literal: Their dress resembles Wusun.
translations.idiomatic: Costume like Wusun's.

sentences.zh: 康居國去長安萬二千三百里，不屬都護。

translations.literal: The state of Kangju is twelve thousand three hundred li from Chang'an and does not belong to the protector-general.
translations.idiomatic: Kangju: 12,300 li from Chang'an, outside the protector-general's register.

sentences.zh: 叵猶遂也。

translations.literal: Po means 'thereupon' or 'finally.'
translations.idiomatic: The character carries the sense of 'thereupon' or 'finally' in pacifying the states.

sentences.zh: 谷吉，長安人，永之父也。

translations.literal: Gu Ji was a man of Chang'an, Yong's father.
translations.idiomatic: Gu Ji of Chang'an was Gu Yong's father.

sentences.zh: 元帝時為□司馬，使送郅支單于侍子，為郅支所殺。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Yuan he served as [court] major and was sent to escort the heir of Chanyu Zhizhi; Zhizhi killed him.
translations.idiomatic: As court major under Yuan he escorted Zhizhi's hostage and was murdered by Zhizhi.

sentences.zh: 張騫，武帝時為郎，使月氏，為匈奴所閉，留之十餘歲，乃亡走大宛，窮急即射禽獸給食。

translations.literal: Zhang Qian, under Emperor Wu, was a gentleman and sent to the Yuezhi; the Xiongnu blocked him and detained him more than ten years; he then fled to Dawan and, pressed by want, shot birds and beasts for food.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qian, detained by the Xiongnu a decade en route to the Yuezhi, escaped to Dawan and lived by hunting.

sentences.zh: 魏絳，晉大夫。

translations.literal: Wei Jiang was a grandee of Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Jiang of Jin.

sentences.zh: 晉悼公時，山戎使孟樂如晉，因魏絳納虎豹之皮，請和諸戎。

translations.literal: Under Duke Dao of Jin the Mountain Rong sent Meng Yue to Jin; through Wei Jiang they offered tiger and leopard skins and asked peace with the Rong.
translations.idiomatic: The Rong sent Meng Yue with pelts to sue for peace through Wei Jiang.

sentences.zh: 公悅，使魏絳盟諸戎。

translations.literal: The duke was pleased and had Wei Jiang covenant the various Rong.
translations.idiomatic: The duke had Wei Jiang swear peace with the Rong tribes.

sentences.zh: 事見左傳。

translations.literal: The affair is seen in the Zuo commentary.
translations.idiomatic: See Zuo zhuan.

sentences.zh: 輯亦和也。

translations.literal: Ji also means 'harmonize.'
translations.idiomatic: Here the verb means to gather people in harmony.

sentences.zh: 賈誼曰：「莫邪為鈍兮，鈆刀為銛。

translations.literal: Jia Yi said: 'Though Moye be dull, the lead knife is sharp.'
translations.idiomatic: Jia Yi: even dull Moye beats a sharp lead blade—irony on worth.

sentences.zh: 」楚詞曰：「捐□太阿，寶鈆刀兮。」

translations.literal: The Chu ci says: 'Cast away the Grand E—treasure the lead blade!'
translations.idiomatic: Chu ci variant on valuing the mean tool.

sentences.zh: 《前書》曰，漢遣公主為烏孫夫人，結為昆弟，則是斷匈奴右臂也。

translations.literal: The Former Han says: Han sent a princess to be consort of Wusun, binding brotherhood—thus cutting the Xiongnu's right arm.
translations.idiomatic: Han shu: the Wusun marriage was called severing the Xiongnu's right arm.

sentences.zh: 哀帝時劉歆上議曰，武帝時立五屬國，起朔方，伐朝鮮，起玄菟、樂浪，以斷匈奴之左臂。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Ai Liu Xin submitted a memorial: under Emperor Wu five dependent states were set up, Shuofang raised, Joseon attacked, Xuantu and Lelang founded—to cut the Xiongnu's left arm.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin: Wu's eastern pushes cut the left arm.

sentences.zh: 西伐大宛，結烏孫，裂匈奴之右臂。

translations.literal: Westward Dawan was struck and Wusun bound—splitting the Xiongnu's right arm.
translations.idiomatic: Western campaigns cut the right arm.

sentences.zh: 南面以西為右也。

translations.literal: Facing south, the west counts as 'right.'
translations.idiomatic: Facing south, west is 'right'—hence 'right arm.'

sentences.zh: 《西域傳》曰「自條支國乘水西行，可百餘日，近日所入」也。

translations.literal: The 'Treatise on the Western Regions' says: 'From Tiaozhi by water westward, more than a hundred days, one nears where the sun sets.'
translations.idiomatic: Western treatise on the sunset lands.

sentences.zh: 效猶驗也。

translations.literal: Xiao means 'verify.'
