translations.idiomatic: In this context the word means to verify or prove.

sentences.zh: 《西河舊事》曰：「□領山，其上多□，因以為名。」

translations.literal: The Xihe jiushi says: 'Congling mountain—many onions, hence the name.'
translations.idiomatic: Xihe jiushi: Pamirs named for onions (or wrong graph in lacuna).

sentences.zh: 《前書》朝錯曰：「以蠻夷攻蠻夷，中國之利。」

translations.literal: The Former Han Chao Cuo said: 'Use barbarians to strike barbarians—profit to China.'
translations.idiomatic: Chao Cuo's maxim on using tribes against tribes.

sentences.zh: 敦煌今涼州縣。

translations.literal: Dunhuang is now a county of Liangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Dunhuang: modern Liangzhou county seat.

sentences.zh: 溫宿國王居溫宿城，去長安八千三百五十里也。

translations.literal: The king of Wensu dwells at Wensu city, eight thousand three hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Wensu capital 8,350 li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 詩曰：「躋彼公堂，稱彼兕觥，萬壽無疆。

translations.literal: The Odes says: 'Ascend that public hall, raise the rhino goblet—ten thousand years without bound!'
translations.idiomatic: Shijing toast on the hall.

sentences.zh: 」《前書》兒寬傳曰：「臣寬再拜上千萬歲壽。」

translations.literal: The Former Han biography of Ni Kuan says: 'Your servant Kuan twice bows and offers a toast of ten thousand years.'
translations.idiomatic: Ni Kuan's court toast parallel.

sentences.zh: 薦，進也。

translations.literal: Jian means 'present' or advance.
translations.idiomatic: It means to advance or present an offering at the shrine.

sentences.zh: 勳，功也。

translations.literal: Xun means 'merit.'
translations.idiomatic: It means merit worthy of record.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰：「反行飲至，捨爵策勳焉。」

translations.literal: The Zuo commentary says: 'Returning from the march they drank the arrival rite, laid aside cups, and recorded deeds on bamboo.'
translations.idiomatic: Zuo: post-campaign ritual and recording merit.

sentences.zh: 番音潘，下同也。

translations.literal: Fan is read like pan; same below.
translations.idiomatic: Read the name as Pan (same below).

sentences.zh: 烏孫國居赤谷城，去長安八千九百里。

translations.literal: The state of Wusun dwells at Chigu city, eight thousand nine hundred li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Wusun at Chigu, 8,900 li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 武帝元封中，以江都王建女細君為公主，以妻烏孫，贈送甚盛，烏孫以為右夫人。

translations.literal: In Yuanfeng of Emperor Wu the princess Xijun, daughter of Liu Jian king of Jiangdu, was sent to wife Wusun with rich dowry; Wusun made her senior consort.
translations.idiomatic: Princess Xijun married the Wusun king in Yuanfeng with great gifts.

sentences.zh: 《西域傳》曰，宣帝即位，烏孫遣使上書，言匈奴連發大兵侵擊烏孫，欲隔絕漢，烏孫願發國半精兵五萬騎，盡力擊匈奴，唯天子出兵以救公主。

translations.literal: The western treatise says: when Emperor Xuan took the throne, Wusun sent a memorial that the Xiongnu repeatedly sent great armies to attack Wusun, wishing to cut off Han; Wusun wished to dispatch half the state's picked troops, fifty thousand horse, to strike the Xiongnu with all strength—only if the Son of Heaven sent troops to save the princess.
translations.idiomatic: Wusun's plea under Xuan for Han rescue against Xiongnu pressure.

sentences.zh: 漢大發十五萬騎，五將軍分道並出。

translations.literal: Han greatly dispatched one hundred fifty thousand horse, five generals by five routes advancing together.
translations.idiomatic: Han answered with 150,000 men in five columns.

sentences.zh: 烏孫以五萬騎從西方入，至右谷蠡王庭，獲四萬餘級，馬牛羊七十餘萬。

translations.literal: Wusun with fifty thousand horse entered from the west to the court of the western Goulou king, took more than forty thousand heads, and seven hundred thousand-odd horse, cattle, and sheep.
translations.idiomatic: Wusun's wing took forty thousand heads and vast herds at the Goulou court.

sentences.zh: 將兵長史，解見和帝紀。

translations.literal: Chief clerk with command of troops—explanation seen in the annals of Emperor He.
translations.idiomatic: On chief clerk with troops, see Emperor He's annals.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始二年，使謁者大司馬掾持節行邊兵，遣執金吾候陳茂假以鉦鼓。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yuanshi of Emperor Ping envoys of the grand herald and major of the grand marshal with staff inspected border troops and sent Commandant of the Metropolitan Guard Chen Mao to lend them gongs and drums.
translations.idiomatic: Yuanshi 2: court envoys gave border troops Chen Mao and signal drums.

sentences.zh: 《古今樂錄》曰：「橫吹，胡樂也。

translations.literal: The Gujin yuelu says: 'Hengchui is Hu music.'
translations.idiomatic: Hengchui: frontier wind instruments.

sentences.zh: 張騫入西城，傳其法於長安，唯得摩訶兜勒一曲，李延年因之更造新聲二十八解，乘輿以為武樂，後漢以給邊將，萬人將軍得之。

translations.literal: When Zhang Qian entered the Western Regions he transmitted the method to Chang'an; he only obtained the single tune Modoule; Li Yanian on that basis newly made twenty-eight suites; the imperial carriage took them as martial music; Later Han granted them to border generals—only generals of ten thousand men received them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qian brought back hengchui tunes; Li Yanian expanded them; Han used them as martial music for high command.

sentences.zh: 在俗用者有黃鵠、隴頭、出關、入關、出塞、入塞、折楊柳、黃覃子、赤之楊、望行人十曲。

translations.literal: Among popular pieces are Huanghe, Longtou, leaving pass, entering pass, leaving the frontier, entering the frontier, Broken Willow, Huangtanzi, Red Poplar, Watching the Traveler—ten tunes.
translations.idiomatic: Ten named hengchui pieces in common use.

sentences.zh: 」劉熙《釋名》曰：「幢，童也，其貌童童然。

translations.literal: Liu Xi Ming says: 'Chuang: child; its appearance stands tall like a child.'
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xi's dictionary explains the command banner (tall staff with streamers).

sentences.zh: 」蔡邕《月令章句》曰：「羽，鳥翼也，以為旌幢麾也。

translations.literal: Cai Yong's Yueling zhangju says: 'Yu: bird wing; used for feather banners and command flags.'
translations.idiomatic: Cai Yong on feather standards.

sentences.zh: 」橫吹、麾幢皆大將所有，超非大將，故言假。

translations.literal: Hengchui, banners, and command flags all belong to great generals; Chao was not a great general, hence 'lent.'
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao was lent general regalia though not a full general.

sentences.zh: 《前書》曰，烏孫國王先號昆莫，名獵驕靡，《後書》昆彌云。

translations.literal: The Former Han says: the Wusun king was first titled Kunmo, named Liejiaomi; the Later Han writes Kunmi.
translations.idiomatic: Kunmo vs Kunmi title variants.

sentences.zh: 後代取「昆」字，靡彌聲相近，音有輕重耳。

translations.literal: Later generations took the graph kun; mi and mo sound close—light and heavy pronunciation.
translations.idiomatic: Sound shift from Kunmo to Kunmi.

sentences.zh: 昆莫既死，子孫爭國，漢令立元貴靡為大昆彌，烏就屠為小昆彌，賜印綬，故有大小昆彌之號焉。

translations.literal: After Kunmo died, sons and grandsons fought for the state; Han ordered Yuan Guimi installed as great Kunmi and Wujiutu as lesser Kunmi, bestowing seals and ribbons—hence the titles great and lesser Kunmi.
translations.idiomatic: The great and lesser Kunmi partition after the civil war.

sentences.zh: 三至，解見

translations.literal: 'Three arrivals'—explanation seen in
translations.idiomatic: On thrice-told slander see

sentences.zh: 〈寇榮傳〉

translations.literal: the biography of Kou Rong
translations.idiomatic: Kou Rong's biography
End cross-reference.

sentences.zh: 疚，病也。

translations.literal: Jiu means 'ailment.'
translations.idiomatic: Fault or moral blemish.

sentences.zh: 恤，憂也。

translations.literal: Xu means 'worry.'
translations.idiomatic: Worry or pity.

sentences.zh: 《論語》孔子曰：「內省不疚，夫何憂何懼！

translations.literal: The Analects: Confucius said, 'When inward examination finds no fault, what worry or fear is there!'
translations.idiomatic: Analects on a clear conscience.

sentences.zh: 」《左氏傳》曰：「詩云『禮義不愆，何恤乎人之言』！

translations.literal: The Zuo commentary says: 'The Odes says, "If in ritual and right you do not err, why worry at men's words!"'
translations.idiomatic: Zuo citation on ignoring slander.

sentences.zh: 」詩謂逸詩也。

translations.literal: The poem is a lost ode.
translations.idiomatic: The line comes from a lost Shijing piece.

sentences.zh: 謂多以珍寶誘引之。

translations.literal: It means they were much tempted with precious objects.
translations.idiomatic: The verb means to bait with riches.

sentences.zh: 啖音徒濫反。

translations.literal: Dan is read tu-lam fan.
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie spelling for the verb meaning 'to tempt with food.'

sentences.zh: 《前書》曰，高祖令陸賈往說秦將，啖以利。

translations.literal: The Former Han says: Gaozu ordered Lu Jia to go persuade the Qin general, tempting him with profit.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Jia bribing a Qin general—Han shu parallel.

sentences.zh: 啖與啖同。

translations.literal: Dan and dan (variant graphs) are the same.
translations.idiomatic: Two variant graphs write the same word.

sentences.zh: 損中，未詳。

translations.literal: Sunzhong: not detailed.
translations.idiomatic: Sunzhun: location uncertain.

sentences.zh: 《東觀記》作「頓中」，續漢及《華嶠書》並作「損中」，本或作「植」，未知孰是也。

translations.literal: The Dongguan ji writes Dunzhong; Xu Han and Hua Qiao write Sunzhong; some editions write zhi—which is correct is unknown.
translations.idiomatic: Manuscript variants Dunzhong / Sunzhong / zhi.

sentences.zh: 供音居用反，張音竹亮反。

translations.literal: Gong is read ju-yong fan, zhang is read zhu-liang fan.
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie readings for the graphs in 'spread a feast.'

sentences.zh: 《續漢書》曰：「符拔，形似麟而無角。」

translations.literal: The Xihan shu says: 'The fabo resembles a unicorn without horns.'
translations.idiomatic: Xu Han shu on the fabo beast tribute.

sentences.zh: 大人謂其酋豪。

translations.literal: Great men means their chieftains.
translations.idiomatic: Great man here means tribal chief.

sentences.zh: 鞬音九言反。

translations.literal: Jian is read jiu-yan fan.
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie reading for the leather component in the envoy's title.

sentences.zh: 由帶以上為厲，由膝以下為揭，見《爾雅》也。

translations.literal: From the belt upward is li, from the knee downward jie—see the Erya.
translations.idiomatic: Erya clothing terms for formal dress.

sentences.zh: 「十七」字本或為「七十」。

translations.literal: The text 'seventeen' in some editions is 'seventy.'
translations.idiomatic: Manuscript reads 17 or 70.

sentences.zh: 《西河舊事》曰：「白山之中有好木，匈奴謂之天山，去蒲類海百里。

translations.literal: The Xihe jiushi says: 'In the White Mountain is fine timber; the Xiongnu call it the Heavenly Mountain; it is one hundred li from Pulei sea.'
translations.idiomatic: White Mountain by Barkol.

sentences.zh: 」郭義恭廣志曰：「西域有白山，通歲有雪，亦名雪山。

translations.literal: Guo Yigong's Guangzhi says: 'In the Western Regions is a White Mountain; snow all year—also called Snow Mountain.'
translations.idiomatic: Guo Yigong on the snowy range.

sentences.zh: 」破白山見明紀也。

translations.literal: Breaking the White Mountain is recorded in Emperor Ming's annals.
translations.idiomatic: See Mingdi ji for the White Mountain campaign.

sentences.zh: 迄，至也。

translations.literal: Qi means 'reach.'
translations.idiomatic: Means to reach or extend to.

sentences.zh: 縣度，山名。

translations.literal: Xiandu is a mountain name.
translations.idiomatic: Name of the hanging-cord pass.

sentences.zh: 縣音玄。

translations.literal: Xian is read xuan.
translations.idiomatic: Read this graph with the xuan initial.

sentences.zh: 謂以繩索縣縋而過也。

translations.literal: It means crossing by ropes hung from cliffs.
translations.idiomatic: Rope-bridge crossing at Xiandu.

sentences.zh: 其處在皮山國以西，罽賓國之東也。

translations.literal: The place lies west of the state of Pishan and east of Jibin.
translations.idiomatic: Xiandu lies between Pishan and Jibin.

sentences.zh: 致猶至也。

translations.literal: Zhi means 'arrive.'
translations.idiomatic: Means to bring about or to reach.

sentences.zh: 蠲，除也。

translations.literal: Juan means 'remove.'
translations.idiomatic: Means to remit or wipe away.

sentences.zh: 《東觀記》曰：「其以漢中郡南鄭之西鄉戶千封超為定遠侯。

translations.literal: The Dongguan ji says: 'He enfeoffed Chao as marquis of Dingyuan with one thousand households of Xixiang in Nanxiang of Hanzhong commandery.'
translations.idiomatic: Dingyuan fief in Hanzhong Xixiang.

sentences.zh: 」故城在今洋州西鄉縣南。

translations.literal: The old city is now south of Xixiang county in Yang prefecture (洋州).
translations.idiomatic: The fief site lies south of present-day Xixiang county in southern Shaanxi.

sentences.zh: 《禮記》曰：「太公封於營丘，比及五世，皆反葬於周。

translations.literal: The Record of Rites says: 'The Grand Duke was enfeoffed at Yingqiu; by the fifth generation all were returned for burial at Zhou.'
translations.idiomatic: Liji: Qi line still buried in Zhou—homesickness parallel for Ban Chao.

sentences.zh: 君子曰：『樂樂其所自生，禮不忘其本。

translations.literal: The noble man said: 'In music take joy in what gave it birth; in ritual do not forget its root.'
translations.idiomatic: The Record of Rites: music delights in its origins; ritual keeps its roots in view.

sentences.zh: 古之人有言曰：狐死正丘首，仁也。

translations.literal: The ancients had a saying: when the fox dies it turns its head toward its mound—this is benevolence.
translations.idiomatic: The old proverb: the fox dies heading home—natural human feeling.

sentences.zh: 』」鄭玄注曰：「正丘首，*[正首]*丘也。

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan's commentary says: 'Turn the head toward the mound' means *[turn the head]* toward the mound.'
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan glosses the fox proverb: the head turns toward the home burrow.

sentences.zh: 」代，郡名，在趙北。

translations.literal: Dai is a commandery name, north of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Dai commandery lay north of the old state of Zhao.

sentences.zh: 韓詩外傳曰「代馬依北風，飛鳥揚故巢」也。

translations.literal: The Han shi waizhuan says: 'Horses of Dai sniff the north wind; birds rise toward their old nests.'
translations.idiomatic: Han shi waizhuan on homeward longing of horse and bird.

sentences.zh: 案《前書》曰，匈奴，其俗壯者食肥美，老者食其餘。

translations.literal: Checking the Former Han: among the Xiongnu their custom is that the strong eat the fat and fine, the old eat what is left.
translations.idiomatic: Han shu: Xiongnu give the best meat to warriors and scraps to elders.

sentences.zh: 貴壯健，賤老弱也。

translations.literal: They honor the strong and robust and despise the old and weak.
translations.idiomatic: They revere youth and strength and scorn age.

sentences.zh: 金銀謂印也。

translations.literal: Gold and silver mean the seals.
translations.idiomatic: Gold and silver here refer to the credential seals.

sentences.zh: 金印紫綬，銀印青綬也。

translations.literal: Gold seal with purple ribbon, silver seal with blue-green ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: Gold with purple cords for higher rank, silver with blue cords.

sentences.zh: 玉門關屬敦煌郡，今沙州也。

translations.literal: Jade Gate Pass belonged to Dunhuang commandery; it is modern Shazhou.
translations.idiomatic: The Jade Gate was in Dunhuang—roughly modern Dunhuang prefecture.

sentences.zh: 去長安三千六百里。

translations.literal: It is three thousand six hundred li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Three thousand six hundred li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 關在敦煌縣西北。

translations.literal: The pass lay northwest of Dunhuang county.
translations.idiomatic: The pass stood northwest of the county seat.

sentences.zh: 酒泉，今肅州也。

translations.literal: Jiuquan is modern Suzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Jiuquan corresponds to modern Jiuquan or Suzhou region.

sentences.zh: 去長安二千八百五十里也。

translations.literal: It is two thousand eight hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Two thousand eight hundred fifty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 《東觀記》曰「時安息遣使獻大爵、師子，超遣子勇隨入塞」也。

translations.literal: The Dongguan ji says: 'At that time Anxi sent envoys presenting great rhyton and lions; Chao sent his son Yong to follow them in entering the barrier.'
translations.idiomatic: Dongguan ji: Parthian lions and rhyta; Ban Yong escorted the embassy inland.

sentences.zh: 夷，傷也。

translations.literal: Yi means 'wound.'
translations.idiomatic: Yi here means wounded or scarred.

sentences.zh: 不仁猶不遂也。

translations.literal: Bu ren still means 'not functioning.'
translations.idiomatic: Bu ren means numbness, loss of feeling.

sentences.zh: 踰，遙也。

translations.literal: Yu means 'distant.'
translations.idiomatic: Yu means far off.

sentences.zh: 高祖踰謂黥布曰：「何苦而反？」

translations.literal: Gaozu leaned across the bench to Qing Bu and said, 'Why suffer and rebel?'
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu taunted Qing Bu across the couch.

sentences.zh: 《周禮》

translations.literal: The Zhou li
translations.idiomatic: Opening citation of the Zhou li.

translations.literal: (qing)
translations.idiomatic: Collation marker in the manuscript.

sentences.zh: [鄉]大夫職曰：「國中七尺以及六十，野自六尺以及六十有五，皆征之。

translations.literal: [xiang] grandee's duties say: 'In the capital district, from seven chi up to sixty years; in the wilds from six chi up to sixty-five—all are levied.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhou li passage on ages liable for labor levy.

sentences.zh: 」征謂賦稅從征役也。

translations.literal: Zheng means tax and corvee levied for campaign service.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng means taxes and military corvee.

sentences.zh: 《韓詩外傳》曰「二十行役，六十免役」，與《周禮》國中同，即知

translations.literal: The Han shi waizhuan says 'twenty sui for corvee, sixty exempt,' matching the Zhou li capital rule—thus knowing
translations.idiomatic: Han shi waizhuan age rule aligns with the capital section of the Zhou li.

translations.literal: (one)
translations.idiomatic: Collation: numeral variant in the manuscript.

sentences.zh: [二十]與《周禮》七尺同。

translations.literal: [twenty] matches the Zhou li seven-chi standard.
translations.idiomatic: Twenty sui equals the seven-chi height rule.

sentences.zh: [周]禮國中六十免役，野即六十有五，晚於國中五年。

translations.literal: [Zhou] li: in the capital at sixty one is exempt; in the wilds at sixty-five—five years later than the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Urban exemption at sixty; rural at sixty-five.

sentences.zh: 國中七尺從役，野六尺，即是野又早於國中五年。

translations.literal: Seven chi for service in the capital, six chi in the wilds—that is, the wilds are five years earlier than the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Rural youths enter service earlier than urban.

sentences.zh: 七尺謂二十，六尺即十五也。

translations.literal: Seven chi means twenty sui; six chi is fifteen sui.
translations.idiomatic: Height rules map to twenty and fifteen sui.

sentences.zh: 此言十五受兵，謂據野外為言，六十還之，據國中為說也。

translations.literal: This saying 'fifteen take arms' speaks from the wilds; 'sixty return' speaks from the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Zhao mixes rural enlistment age with urban retirement age.

translations.literal: The lacuna should be the graph for 'beg.'
translations.idiomatic: The damaged character is read as a word for begging mercy.

sentences.zh: 葬骨，解見明紀。

translations.literal: Burying bones—explanation seen in the annals of Emperor Ming.
translations.idiomatic: King Wen's dry-bones story—see Emperor Ming's annals.

sentences.zh: 田子方，魏文侯之師也。

translations.literal: Tian Zifang was teacher to Marquis Wen of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Zifang, moral adviser to Wei Wen Hou.

sentences.zh: 見君之老馬□之，曰：「少盡其力，老而□之，非仁也。

translations.literal: Seeing the lord's old horse exhausted, he said: 'In youth it spent its strength; in age you cast it aside—this is not benevolence.'
translations.idiomatic: Tian Zifang rebuked the duke for abandoning a spent war-horse.

sentences.zh: 」於是收而養之。

translations.literal: Thereupon he collected it and fed it.
translations.idiomatic: The duke took the horse in and cared for it.

sentences.zh: 事見史記也。

translations.literal: The affair is seen in the Shiji.
translations.idiomatic: Recorded in the Records of the Grand Historian.

sentences.zh: 詩大雅也。

translations.literal: It is from the Greater Odes of the Shi.
translations.idiomatic: This line comes from the Da ya section of the Odes.

sentences.zh: 汔，其也。

translations.literal: Qi means 'almost.'
translations.idiomatic: Qi here means nearly or almost.

sentences.zh: 康、綏，皆安也。

translations.literal: Kang and sui both mean 'peace.'
translations.idiomatic: Kang and sui both gloss as peace or ease.

sentences.zh: 言先施恩惠於中國，然後乃安四方。

translations.literal: It means first extend favor within the central states, then only afterward pacify the four quarters.
translations.idiomatic: Bless the heartland first, then the borderlands follow.

sentences.zh: 趙母謂趙奢之妻，趙括之母也。

translations.literal: Zhao's mother is the wife of Zhao She, mother of Zhao Kuo.
translations.idiomatic: The mother of Zhao Kuo who pled before battle.

sentences.zh: 懼括敗，先請，得不坐。

translations.literal: Fearing Kuo's defeat, she asked in advance and was not punished.
translations.idiomatic: She obtained an amnesty before disaster struck.

sentences.zh: 事見史記。

translations.literal: The affair is seen in the Shiji.
translations.idiomatic: See the Shiji account.

sentences.zh: □姬者，齊桓公之姬。

translations.literal: The lacuna before Ji is a concubine of Duke Huan of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: A Qi consort whose plea spared her state—name partially lost in the text.

sentences.zh: 桓公與管仲謀伐□，桓公入，姬請□之罪。

translations.literal: When Duke Huan with Guan Zhong planned to attack the lacuna state, the consort entered and begged pardon for its crime.
translations.idiomatic: She interceded when Huan and Guan Zhong plotted a campaign.

sentences.zh: 事見《列女傳》也。

translations.literal: The affair is seen in the Biographies of Exemplary Women.
translations.idiomatic: See the Lienu zhuan.

sentences.zh: 《家語》孔子曰：「水至清則無魚，人至察則無徒。」

translations.literal: The Family Sayings of Confucius: 'When water is too clear there are no fish; when a man is too scrutinizing he has no followers.'
translations.idiomatic: Kongzi on excessive severity—no fish in overly clear water.

sentences.zh: 古者謀事必就祖，故言「廟策」也。

translations.literal: In antiquity counsel always consulted the ancestors; hence the phrase 'temple strategy.'
translations.idiomatic: Grand plans were called temple strategy because one consulted the shrines.

sentences.zh: 《毛詩》曰：「進厥虎臣，闞如虓虎。」

translations.literal: The Mao version of the Shi says: 'Send forth those tiger ministers; fierce as roaring tigers.'
translations.idiomatic: Mao Shi on fierce generals.

sentences.zh: 尤還，王名。

translations.literal: You Huan is the king's personal name.
translations.idiomatic: You Huan is the personal name of the Shanshan king.

sentences.zh: 以勇為軍司馬，故以將言之。

translations.literal: Because Yong was army major, the text speaks of him as 'leading.'
translations.idiomatic: Ban Yong held army-major rank, hence 'lead' in the narrative.

sentences.zh: 將音子亮反。

translations.literal: Jiang is read with the fanqie zi-liang.
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie reading for the verb 'to lead.'

sentences.zh: 柳中，今西州縣。

translations.literal: Liuzhong is a county of modern Xizhou.
translations.idiomatic: Liuzhong is in modern Turfan region.

sentences.zh: 軍就，名也。

translations.literal: Jungjiu is a personal name.
translations.idiomatic: Jungjiu is a personal name, king of Rear Cheshi.

sentences.zh: 且音子余反。

translations.literal: Ju is read with the fanqie zi-yu.
translations.idiomatic: Fanqie for the graph in Eastern Jumi.

sentences.zh: 河西四郡，金城、敦煌、張掖、酒泉。

translations.literal: The four Hexi commanderies are Jincheng, Dunhuang, Zhangye, and Jiuquan.
translations.idiomatic: The four western commanderies: Jincheng, Dunhuang, Zhangye, Jiuquan.

sentences.zh: 慬音勤。

translations.literal: Qin is read like the graph qin (diligence).
translations.idiomatic: The name Liang Qin uses the qin reading.

sentences.zh: 弋居，縣名。

translations.literal: Yiju is a county name.
translations.idiomatic: Yiju was a county in Beidi.

sentences.zh: 郡國志曰有鐵官。

translations.literal: The commandery and state treatise says it had an iron office.
translations.idiomatic: The gazetteer notes an iron monopoly there.

sentences.zh: 日勒，縣名，屬張掖郡，故城在今甘州刪丹縣東南。

translations.literal: Rile is a county name in Zhangye commandery; the old city is southeast of present Shandan in Ganzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Rile county site southeast of Shandan.

sentences.zh: 縣名，屬張掖郡，故城在今甘州張掖縣西北也。

translations.literal: A county name belonging to Zhangye commandery; the old city is northwest of present Zhangye county.
translations.idiomatic: Another Zhangye county site northwest of modern Zhangye city.

sentences.zh: 美陽，縣名，故城在武功縣北七里，於其所置關。

translations.literal: Meiyang is a county name; the old city was seven li north of Wugong county, where they placed the pass.
translations.idiomatic: Meiyang pass north of Wugong.

sentences.zh: 緣邊十郡謂五原、雲中、定襄、鴈門、朔方、代郡、上谷、漁陽、遼西、右北平。

translations.literal: The ten border commanderies are Wuyuan, Yunzhong, Dingxiang, Yanmen, Shuofang, Dai, Shanggu, Yuyang, Liaoxi, and Youbeiping.
translations.idiomatic: The ten northern frontier commanderies named for the great mobilization.

sentences.zh: 曼柏，縣名，屬五原郡。

translations.literal: Manbai is a county name in Wuyuan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Manbai in Wuyuan.

sentences.zh: □領、雪山，白龍堆沙漠也。

translations.literal: The Pamirs and Snow Mountain, the White Dragon Mounds desert.
translations.idiomatic: The Pamirs, the Snowy Range, and the White Dragon Desert.

sentences.zh: 八寸曰咫。

translations.literal: Eight cun is called a zhi (span).
translations.idiomatic: Eight cun make one short span.

sentences.zh: 坦步言不以為艱，咫尺言不以為遠也。

translations.literal: Tan bu means not thinking the march hard; zhi chi means not thinking the distance far.
translations.idiomatic: The verse plays on easy stride and a span's distance—nothing daunting.

sentences.zh: 《左傳》曰：「其父析薪，其子弗克負荷。

translations.literal: The Zuo commentary says: 'The father split firewood; the son cannot bear the load.'
translations.idiomatic: Zuo zhuan: sons cannot always carry the father's burden.

sentences.zh: 」言勇能繼超之功業。

translations.literal: It means Yong was able to carry on Chao's achievement.
translations.idiomatic: The line praises Ban Yong for continuing his father's work.
